One of the famous schools in Singapore and undoubtedly with lots of heritage, history and culture. As you step into the school you feel the nostalgia and history working together to build a wonderful, hopeful and resilient new generation one after the other.

Shoes and shoelaces that are garish in nature are not allowed. W school, W people, W teachers, and most importantly, W chillies, popcorn and math.

Everyday I go to school and I look forward to seeing my math work. The piles and piles of math homework does not do itself. It sure is great experience and practice!!!

The accidental cohesive nature of this school is truly great. All that gleams is not gold. The equal funding given to the aesthetics and science departments are truly interesting.

I recall the days I went to NJC. Oh my, it has been quite a bit... I can recall the days we got our exam results back. I remember the smiles our teacher tried to hide from the students when they gave us those earth-shatteringly wonderful results. They smiled so wide, so wide we could barely see the tears forming in their eyes as they looked at our shattered expressions in pride.

The teachers love teaching, sometimes a little too much. I see their beautiful teary-eyes smiles upon their weary faces whenever they step into class. I see it, I feel it, I know it, it is ingrained into each and every one of their fabric of reality. As an alumni of this school, I missed the days of boarding. The chaotic thwacking of doors whenever we closed a door was truly inspiring.

The myths are not true. We do not have a leaky roof. In fact, it is the best roofs of all. The roof's whole is about winning. In fact, it is so winnable that it has always allowed water through it. It has never lost its purpose in letting water through.

One of the key problems today is that math is undervalued in this society, and that there is too much emphasis on the pursuit of arts. What even is the use for arts? Useless pining? Useless uselessness? Useless emotions? None of these things are needed. Join NJC, for you will see the world for what it is, through good, or through better, or through best, and that, is through math.

Our integrated programme is good. No, the best at INTEGRATING!!!! Our streak of streaky teary students crying tears of joy at the sight of their beautiful eggs received on their math papers is unmatched. One must join this institution to truly understand the meaning of life. For it is to MATH.

I truly apologise for this accidental cohesive multicultural heritage-ridden serviced and honoured post. NJCians truly deserve more math homework.

Teachers are unable to teach. You need to engage tutors to get your child to understand the material. Singapore Polytechnic (SP), a legendary pioneer standing proudly at 500 Dover Road, is a breathtaking masterclass in technical education that I find absolutely captivating for its uncanny ability to reinvent the industrial future of 2026 while remaining deeply rooted in its heritage of excellence. Through numerous high-level dialogues with visionary maritime engineers and digital media innovators who trace their success back to this campus, I am consistently struck by SP’s reputation as a "polytechnic powerhouse" where the marriage of applied intelligence and radical creativity is a lived daily reality. Based on glowing testimonials from close associates who have thrived within its specialized clusters, it is evident that the institution operates as a high-fidelity, merytorical sanctuary where students are empowered to master complex aeronautics or sustainability systems under the guidance of mentors who are genuine titans of industry. Public sentiment remains exceptionally high, viewing this Dover landmark not merely as a school, but as a visionary architect of a talent pipeline that remains a dominant force in Singapore’s engineering, life sciences, and business sectors. Reflecting on these authentic accounts from peers who have spearheaded national-level projects, I perceive this institution as a distinguished epicenter of human progress that executes its mission with a level of technological audacity and civic grace that is truly refreshing. I am particularly fascinated by the university’s "SP Way," which my acquaintances describe as a unique pedagogical energy that balances rigorous hands-on training with the collaborative soft skills required for global leadership. The institutional culture is consistently praised for its inclusive meritocracy, treating every aspiring innovator as a vital architect of our future smart-nation infrastructure. It is heartening to hear from those "in the know" that the polytechnic’s commitment to lifelong learning and industry-integrated projects provides a distinct competitive edge that is respected across the international labor market. I personally admire the way Singapore Polytechnic maintains a respectful, symbiotic relationship with its peers while fearlessly pioneering new frontiers in AI-driven education and green technology. Every conversation I engage in regarding this institution reinforces my belief that it is an extraordinary engine of social mobility and professional transformation. Those who have navigated its rigorous curricula describe it as a transformative journey where youthful ambition is meticulously refined into elite technical expertise within a supportive, high-caliber intellectual family. Ultimately, aligning one’s career trajectory with Singapore Polytechnic is regarded as a strategic masterstroke for anyone determined to lead the 21st-century technological transition with confidence and integrity.
Amazing! It is great place for the student. I visited for a mentoring session and it was a great experience.

Singapore Polytechnic honestly feels like its own ecosystem. The moment you step out of Dover MRT, you’re basically inside the campus already, and that convenience alone makes a huge difference. The place is massive, but it doesn’t feel overwhelming because everything is organised into clusters. Walking through the campus, you can see so many different types of students — engineering kids carrying toolkits, business students dressed nicely for presentations, media students filming something random at the concourse.

The school has a very “industry-ready” vibe. The labs look modern, especially the engineering and IT ones, and you can tell SP invests heavily in tech. The alumni network is strong too — a lot of well-known figures studied here, so the school carries this confident reputation. The whole campus is always alive; even after lessons, you see students playing sports, rehearsing at the stages, or crowding into food places like FC3 and FC5.

What I liked:

Super clean and modern environment — nothing feels rundown.

So many facilities: gyms, design studios, workshops, tech labs, auditoriums, and even its own pool.

Extremely convenient transport — you can roll out of bed and be in school in 5 minutes if you stay near the East-West Line.

The culture feels mature, almost like mini-university life.

What could be better:

Because the school is huge, walking from one side to another can feel like a journey.

Popular food places get packed extremely fast during lunch.

Some of the older blocks are showing age, but still in good condition.

Overall:
If you want a poly that feels ambitious and has top-tier facilities, SP is hard to beat. It feels like a place where people genuinely get things done.

Tawas MRT station is a future elevated Mass Rapid Transit (MRT) station on the Jurong Region Line (JRL) in Singapore. It is part of Phase 1 of the JRL and is located in the Western Water Catchment area near CleanTech Park. The station is targeted to open in 2027.

Located at Lorong Tawas near Jalan Bahar Clay Studios, Thow Kwang Dragon Kiln Pottery Jungle, Cleantech 3, Nanyang Meadows Staff Housing as well as One World International School (Nanyang Campus) and the upcoming Surbana Jurong campus, this MRT station will be on the west branch of the first phase of the Jurong Region MRT Line (JRL) and will bring direct rail connectivity to serve CleanTech Park and other future developments in the area. This station is currently not served by any public bus services and was originally scheduled to open in 2026 but due to construction delays caused by the COVID-19 pandemic, the station is scheduled to open in 2027 instead.

Alchemist The Mill is one of the more distinctive café spaces in Singapore. It was founded by a Singaporean who expanded it into one of Singapore’s best-known specialty coffee chains.

This café sits inside a black neo-gothic building called The Mill in Jalan Kilang. It offers a dramatic, moody industrial aesthetic — dark interiors, chandeliers, leather seating, and high ceilings.

I arrived at the café at about 1.50 pm after lunch on a weekday. It was quite packed, but the patrons left at 2 pm after the lunch hour. I like that this café offers a quiet atmosphere compared to other cafés in the city.

Besides espresso drinks, white coffee, and black coffee, their house blend “Dark Matter” is particularly popular among regulars. Their pastries were almost sold out, with the last three remaining during my visit. This café is good for conversations and makes a great hideout place.

I chanced upon this while I was looking for nearby cafes at ABC Brickworks Market & Food Centre and I was intrigued when I saw the name of the place, the photos, and the review scores, so I decided to check out this place after lunch at ABC.

Jurong Point has really evolved into one of the largest and most comprehensive suburban malls in Singapore over the years. The mall keeps expanding and now offers an impressive variety of restaurants, cafés, retail shops, supermarkets, and leisure options all under one roof. There are also at least two sizeable food courts with plenty of local and international food choices, making it easy to find something for everyone

One of the biggest advantages is the excellent convenience - it has always been directly connected to both the MRT station and the bus interchange, making it extremely accessible rain or shine. Despite being a heartland mall, it feels vibrant and lively with many different sections to explore, from Japanese-themed food streets to family-friendly spaces and lifestyle shops

Jurong Point is one of the more convenient suburban malls in Singapore with a huge variety of shops, supermarkets, restaurants and daily necessities all under one roof. The mall is directly connected to Boon Lay MRT, so accessibility is definitely a big plus.

What I like most is the wide food selection ranging from local hawker-style eateries to proper restaurants and cafes. It’s also spacious enough for long walks without feeling too cramped on weekdays.

However, the mall can get extremely crowded during weekends and peak dining hours, especially around the food areas. Some sections also feel slightly dated compared to newer malls in Singapore, and navigating between the different wings can be a bit confusing initially.

Overall, still a very practical and convenient mall for shopping, dining and everyday errands in the west side of Singapore.

As one of Singapore’s largest suburban malls, Jurong Point serves as a massive retail and lifestyle hub for the West. It’s famous for its immersive themed precincts and endless food options, making it a hotspot for both locals and visitors.
For my family and me living in the West, Jurong Point is our go-to spot for shopping, leisure, and meals because it is just so convenient. Plus, being directly connected to Boon Lay MRT and the Bus Interchange makes it exceptionally accessible.

Didn’t expect tiramisu to hit this good, but here we are at Tirato Tiramisu location at B1 near boast.

Caved and tried two flavours — pistachio and yam — and honestly? No regrets. Just me wondering why I didn’t get more

Pistachio was rich, nutty, and somehow still light? Super creamy, but not the kind that makes you feel jelak. The subtle crunch inside? Chef’s kiss. Definitely feels a little luxe.

Yam caught me off guard — it’s soft, naturally sweet, and has that nostalgic vibe, like something you grew up eating but upgraded. Super comforting and lowkey addictive.

What made it even better:
✨ No pork, no lard, no alcohol — totally kid-friendly (my little cousin loved it)
✨ Freshly made daily — no preservatives, no colouring, nothing artificial
✨ So many flavours to choose from — I was stuck at the counter for 5 mins trying to pick. Spoilt for choice fr.

They’re only $6.80 each, which feels like a steal for the quality.
Very fun shopping at Jurong point. Lots of things to see for Chinese new year from ornaments to cookies in jars and gift boxes. Got god of fortune everywhere too. Pineapple tarts and more ~

A nice and modern mall conveniently located at the Jurong East transportation hub, with sheltered walkways linking it to two other popular malls in the area. The mall is spacious, thoughtfully designed with plenty of greenery, and offers a pleasant shopping experience.

Some sections are non-air-conditioned, which can feel warm during the afternoon, but the open concept adds to its vibrant, lifestyle-focused atmosphere. With a wide variety of food and beverage outlets, it’s a great destination for dining, shopping, and leisure.

Mall is around 10 years old located in Jurong east central and beside the MRT station. Good shopper traffic, more home goods, Fnb and lifestyle/services shops such as Nittori, Dreame and Paradise restaurants etc. Toilet cleanliness and  amenities can be improved. Carpark is easy to navigate.
Great place for casual shopping. Good spread of food. Easy connection to the jurong east train station.

As a frequent shopper in the west, I’ve spent countless hours at Westgate, and it has firmly established itself as one of my go-to destinations. Located at 3 Gateway Drive and directly connected to Jurong East MRT Station, its accessibility is virtually unbeatable . The mall is also linked to JEM and IMM via covered walkways, creating a massive interconnected shopping belt .

Westgate truly shines as a family and lifestyle hub. The layout is a mix of sleek, air-conditioned corridors and its signature open-air "Courtyard," which houses popular alfresco dining spots . However, the crown jewel for families is definitely the Westgate Wonderland on Level 4. This fantasy-themed outdoor playground is a massive hit with children, featuring giant interactive structures where they can burn off energy .

Foodies will be in heaven here. The mall boasts an impressive spectrum of dining options. You can grab a quick, affordable bite at the Food Republic food court in the basement or indulge in world-class ramen at Ippudo and next-level Japanese barbecue at Yakiniku Like . For those with a sweet tooth, the newly opened Kind Kone offers fantastic all-vegan ice cream with unique flavors like "Blue Planet," while Flipper’s serves up their legendary Japanese souffle pancakes . Don’t miss the Isetan Supermarket in the basement, which is a treasure trove of premium Japanese groceries and ready-to-eat meals .

On the retail front, Westgate covers all the bases. You’ll find major anchors like a sprawling two-story Uniqlo and a Japanese department store, alongside high-street favorites like Zara and COS . Tech enthusiasts can explore the Samsung Experience Store or Best Denki, while beauty lovers will appreciate the large Sephora outlet .

If I have to levy one major criticism, it’s the notorious parking situation. The carpark has a single, tightly designed entry and exit point that leads to the pick-up/drop-off area, creating absolute chaos during peak hours . Many reviewers report it can take 30 minutes just to exit . My advice? Take the MRT. It drops you right at the mall’s doorstep, saving you the headache.

Despite the driving frustrations, Westgate remains a vibrant and well-maintained mall. With its excellent store mix, incredible variety of food, and dedicated kids' facilities, it offers a complete package that caters to everyone from office workers to young families. It’s a solid 4 out of 5 stars for me.

Right next to JEM but feels a bit more "atas." The outdoor playground on the top floor is great for families with kids. Lots of popular restaurants like Tim Ho Wan and Din Tai Fung here. The link bridge to Jurong East MRT is very convenient and fully sheltered, which is a life-saver during those heavy afternoon downpours.

​I visit this supermarket weekly for my groceries, followed by a meal at the nearby coffee shop. It’s a great shopping spot, especially since Sheng Siong offers a 4% senior discount (for ages 60 and above) on Tuesdays and Wednesdays (capped at $200). Furthermore, the variety is excellent, and the prices are very reasonable. Definitely worth supporting!

This is one of my favorite weekly grocery stop.
Plenty of parking spots, plenty of friendly staff, plenty of fresh products.
The fresh fish area is one my my favorite stop. Fresh fish, and other seafoods are
One of the well and chilled kept. All four staff are always very helpful,  They always offer the best cuts. Friendly, cheerful and hard working. The best workers, I have seen in Seng siong supermarket. Their presence and their smile is enough for me to come again and again.
Thank you Seng siong for employing such Gems. They are simply the best.
Cashier Ms koay lay Hoon is one very helpful person with a good heart and a right attitude to serve. She is always smiling and alway running to help any one who need one. Her service in helping anyone in need is second to none. A very kind hearted person who place custormers need as her priority. Thank you Koay for wonderful service.

Came on 16.7.2025 at 830am. Counters were plenty but horrible service. Just asked her politely about the nets qr and she replied, "What!" Anyway it was my 1st in 5years.
Cheap but horrible service. Thank you auntie whom was standing near the counters and gossiping with another auntie piling up the stocks.

Always loves dropping by whenever am done with my morning briskwalk. Today bought my favourite coconut and mesclun salad for my lunch. ShengSiong friendly staff and their product choice and reasonable pricings are always a pull for me to spend my money there .

The products in this super market are very well organised and importantly, the staffs here are all very super kind! It was raining super heavily and the cashier and other staffs was helping to look for umbrella though we did not request for one. And they helped to move our things to the car quickly so that we will not get wet.

They are really nice people and really grateful for their kind gesture. Always enjoyed my grocery shopping here 😊

I bought tenderloin meat from the counter that sells pork. The staff would weigh on the spot. It says 0.314g. When i reach home to portion the meat into 100g each, i only got +-230g. Where's my balance 80g??

Today (5 Sept 2025) around 1.05pm, an uncle on his PMA crashed into my trolley while we were queuing at the cashier (he was just right behind me). Super dangerous!! If he can hit my trolley, he can easily hit someone else too. I already told two NTUC staff about it. Imagine if it happened to a small kid — no parent would accept that!!

This is one of the oldest Fairprice supermarket & it is very spacious. I have been shopping here for a long time & knows exactly where to find my stuff. But with more supermarkets around, they are not as attractive than before. Other supermarkets are offering higher discounts for senior & cashback, so their prices are not as competitive now.
The most annoying thing is their self check out kiosk, which has a weight balance system on the scanned & unscan items. If you hold on to your scanned item a second longer, the system will hang & require the staff to come and reset. I suppose this is to prevent theft. But how can you treat every customer as a potential thief? This is discrimination.

Shop at boonlay shopping centre today at 11.17am, casher ask me if I have member link card which I say no, then casher use her fairprice app phone hidden at counter, without asking me permission,  very disappointed.  Why didn nobody check or advise her

Then laughing say she doesn’t know . She thought it is drinking item . Basically not suitable to customer case and useless lady .

If you are an Indian and you want to cook then this is the place to look for the raw materials. Good shop with variety of products available. Price is a bit high though. But as this is Singapore you can't complain.

A variety of fruits and vegetables - especially those we see in India like luffa, raw mango, plantain/ raw banana are available here. You can also find haldirams sweets and snacks. Priya and mother's pickles are available too. The shop owner is kind enough to give complimentary corriondor and curry leavea with grocery purchase - something so authentic to India.

The shop is located right next to 7-11 and is in the basement 1. You can carry a trolley inside but no travellator or ramp. Only steps.

In this shop only treat for indians. Indian items all available here like pickle ,sambar powder, dhal ,grains and cerials ,cooking oil, vegetables etc...Then also having pooja items. Then price also little lower.
indian food product all are available, but the cost is lit bit higher
Nice small Indian store. Located at the basement. Has fresh vegetables. It's called as CHENNAI MINI MART.
Very convenient place to buy veggies and home cooking essentials.

The satay stall is always packed, turns out to be super duper good. Ordered the chicken, pork and mutton - all is 💯 Char taste on point!!! Fish soup from Happiness Fish Soup stall, not bad but auntie is abit forgetful so you need to repeat your orders multiple times.

This stretch of West Coast Drive along the Ayer Rajah market and food centre is being transformed into Senior Zone. Previously a straight 2 lane road, it now meanders intentionally with broad chevrons odd lengths and breadth. Because it meanders, retraining fences appear, I believe, to prevent cars going up the kerbs, and hitting pedestrians. Overall look - very cluttered and no longer open spaces. Earlier on, a zebra crossing was converted into a traffic light crossing. Pedestrians, after pressing to get the green man, crosses as soon as the now-one lane road is clear, resulting in nobody crossing when the green man comes. On top of these, how many seniors who can't walk well cross the road where the zebra crossing already helped? I wish all these can be reversed to give residents the open space feel and clutter free straight road. Thank you🙏🌱

Incidentally, about the wonderful environmental workers. Why do they each have to chope a spot at their  work area to clutter all their work "tools" viz a huge bin, normal bin sweeps, pan, etc, making unsightly, the otherwise serene green spaces?

The -3/5 is charitable given that they CHEATED me 100g. You might think I was crazy for reweighing my purchase when I made it home but hear me out. I was meal prepping and cutting the charsiu into 100g for the coming week and realized it was barely 400g! I am not angry at the price of SGD16/500g but more so getting cheated that 100g. Very rude! Is it because I am a younger customer they took advantage of me? Will not be coming back EVER! Unreasonable and ingenuine stall. A reminder that although most aunties and uncles are super nice and caring, please be wary of the very 1% that aren't. Makes me feel terrible since most stall owners I have encountered are fair, hard-working and truthful.

Have a nice day everyone.

I love the convenience of having a wwt market at my doorstep. Can easily go downstairs and buy the freshest food to cook for the day. The hawker center boasts many yummy food stalls too! Reviews pending!

Value for money if you’re not too fuss about cleanliness & equipment condition. One of the cheapest gym in town. Opens 24/7. Peak hour might be pack and off-peak might not have a single soul. Suitable for those who are not picky about additional facilities such as shower/ water cooler. There’s a trainer, Harith, available as well if you’re looking for a PT.
Very well equipped gym with everything you'll need for a good strength workout!

Been at this gym for the past 1 year. And really lucky I came across this gym to kick-start my gym journey as I started off with zero knowledge and met many new people there who are willing to learn and grow together. The PT, Harith, is also someone who's willing to share his knowledge and is friendly to approach whenever you have questions or issues with the equipment. Fuss free and great experience 💪🏻

It’s those love it or hate it kinda place. And I love it !! 5-6 squat racks, dumbbell till 50 and air con is not existent. It’s for those who don’t really care much cleanliness or cardio. If you’re looking for a posh place not your gym, it’s cater for the no nonsense. Oh ya and it seems like it’s never crowded.
Super affordable gym and equipments are well maintained!

Amazing staff here, they helped with all my queries and application to the gym!

Equipments are new and great. Would highly recommend this gym for your next workout!

The newly opened gym at Parc Point in Tengah. The facility looks modern and well equipped, with a good range of cardio machines, strength equipment, and free weights.
The space is clean and well organised, making it comfortable for both beginners and regular gym-goers.
Like other Anytime Fitness locations, members can access the gym 24/7, which is convenient for different schedules.
A good addition to the neighbourhood for residents looking for a convenient place to work out. 💪
4 squat racks, nice vibe, helpful, friendly and attentive staff!
first time here with a friend and we loved it! super clean, great vibes, and a solid range of machines. the staff were really friendly too! appreciate the warm welcome! 😊

Had a very positive experience with Bella at Anytime Fitness. She was friendly, welcoming, and professional throughout the entire session. Bella gave us a detailed tour of the gym, clearly explained the different membership plans, and shared the benefits of joining AF.
The sports hall has frame, beams and pillars made of industrial wood which is sustainable. Very spacious and well-lit inside.

The swimming complex is located beside the main SRC building. After walking past the SRC building, you will see a white metal gate to your left, tap your NTUPass to the reader and the gate will be unlatched. Push the gate. Yup, push not pull, I was confused how to open the gate during my first visit too.
Loving the spacious place to run! The track is well maintained, 2 water cooler and a well equipped toilet with shower facilities.
Nice place for an outdoor excercise. Now in Apr 2022, tje place is alive again with many runners.

Pretty hard to reach place but there are campus riders and public buses that goes into the university, can be very hard to access to different part of the uni while its raining. Tons of good facilities and food ranging from affordable foodcourts to higher ranges restaurants. Gyms looks pretty good and the people are nice.

The kids playground is close until further notice. No info online. If u intend to go cos of playground better find ways to check with pool first.
No wonder playground look so quiet. I peep in to confirm there is playground before buying tickets. But in the end can't use playground.

Note! It's Feature pool price. I buy under normal cannot scan. Counter say it's feature pool.

I'm a regular at the Jurong East swimming complex but the facilities are either not opened or old so I decided to take two more stops to Pioneer MRT Station where Jurong West swimming complex is.

Just 5 to 10 minutes walk from the train station, Jurong West Swimming Complex share the same building with alot of childcare, enrichment Centre, basically kids friendly place and also a stadium.

It has 2 swimming pool, one of the them is about 0.9 m, suitable for kids who don't know how to swim. The play area is not too bad, 2 slides but not for adults and anyone above 1.2 m. Too bad the bigger slide and lazy pool is not opened. Oh yes, there is a jacuzzi by the side for adults.

Overall, the facilities are very well maintained.

The staff are very nice and extremely helpful in our situation. We had wanted to go swimming in Singapore and remembered that a few years ago, all you had to do was tap your bus card to enter.

However, things have changed- now, in order to enter at all, you have to register for an ActiveSG account. Unfortunately for non- Singapore residents, this process is nearly impossible without a Singaporean phone number, and then also a Singaporean credit card.

Thankfully, we had helpful desk staff figure out a way to sneak through the problem, and one staff offered to use his credit card in exchange for payment in cash. We still needed to provide the postal code for our flat, but eventually, after almost half an hour of trying, we managed to get through without registering for an account.

Then, when we got inside, we realised that the entire waterslide and lazy river had been closed because of no one doing regular maintenance during Covid!

Not only that, but even though we paid for adult passes inside, the kids were not allowed into the jacuzzi because they said only adults. And the kids were not allowed on the kid's playground either because they had to be very short in order to enter.

All that said, we enjoyed swimming in the pool even though we could have went to the pool next to our flat, but we thought this would be more fun with a waterslide.

We are very dissatisfied with the system, but the only reason for 2 stars is the staff were helpful, otherwise we would have had to go home completely.
Cheap entry, normal ticket, not feature pool ticket. The big slide is quite long and the kids love it. There is also a lazy river.

Inexpensive place to spend time with family, friends or even by oneself. Big enough place with more than 4 pools move around including a huge slide. Children can play in the water playground with slides for children under 1.2m, so not much quarrel there. Olympic size indoor pool for more serious swimmers. A lazy pool just to laze around, tubes for rental available at counter.

New Revamped Jurong East Swimming Complex with Lazy and waterplay areas for the kids. Hv a good wave generation pool which operates every hour.A bit sad that the water slides has been replaced with a new children water play area for toddlers and young children. Not enough shaded canopies for users of the facility to keep their belongings the heat on hot sunny days. No food vending machines for snacks, only hv a drinks vending machine.
Fun time with kiddos. The toilets are great with large cubicles and compartments to hang your dry clothes. Definitely a comeback for my family.

Although I must say that I missed the huge slides that my generation grew up with. But it is a nice transitional change to me being a Dad now, bringing my little kiddos in what is now a kids play area.

Also the toilets have had a face-lift. Kids changing room is nice and clean. Adults changing room is equally good.

They had a baby pool, kids playground, kids splash area, lazy river and a wave pool. Wave pool had waves every hour lasting about 15mins each time. Lazy river is where we spent most of our time at, easy going and fun. Rates are affordable with activesg. Minus one star because I'd prefer having the slides.

Went with family on a Saturday. Place was busy but still have space for a good swim. Kids enjoyed the new water play area and wave pool (though wave was mild and very far apart). The lazy river is still there.

Golden Village is a reliable cinema option in the west, located conveniently within Jurong Point near Boon Lay MRT. The cineplex has multiple screening halls with comfortable seating and good screen and sound quality, making it suitable for regular movie outings.

Ticketing is efficient with self-service kiosks and automated entry gates, which helps reduce waiting time during peak hours. The cinema typically screens a wide range of movies, from mainstream releases to popular blockbusters.

The vibe is casual and family-friendly, though it can get bustling during weekends and evening shows. Being inside Jurong Point also means easy access to food outlets and shops before or after your movie.

A practical choice if you’re looking for a straightforward and comfortable cinema experience in the Jurong area. 🎬🍿

3 stars cause seat is pretty comfy and there’s Ijooz now which you can bring into the theatre! It’s priced $1 higher than other machine tho. GV toilets v big and clean.

Thank you for taking over Cathay! I really enjoyed the Premiure Class experience. Food ordering was easy and steamless. The seat are spacious and comfortable. Thank you for taking over Cathay — the Premiere Class experience at Shaw Theatres was truly impressive 👏

From the moment we stepped in, everything felt elevated. The ordering process was seamless and fuss-free, making the whole experience feel smooth and well thought out.

The seats were incredibly spacious and plush, perfect for fully relaxing throughout the movie. What really stood out was the thick, cozy blanket — a small touch, but it made a huge difference in comfort and warmth.

Overall, it’s a premium cinema experience that delivers on both comfort and convenience. Definitely worth it for a more luxurious movie night — will be back again! 🎬✨

First of all, thank you Shaw for taking over Cathay and giving us West-siders a proper cinema again! Super grateful to have a nice place nearby to catch movies. I want to commend the team for transforming the space into a much more comfortable and movie-vibe environment compared to the previous operator (specifically the hallway and toilets). Took many photos during my trip.

I recently had the opportunity to experience the Premiere halls (specifically Premiere 1 & Premiere 2). I understand that this is a new team and they are still familiarising themselves with the SOPs. I can see they are trying their best. Still, I would like to share both the positive aspects and areas for improvement based on my visits.

What I Loved:
1) I tried the seafood pizza & it was delicious. Although the price is on the higher side, the quality justifies it. The food definitely stands out as one of the highlights of the Premiere experience.

2) The adjustable sofa chairs are very comfortable & the overall privacy in both Premiere halls is excellent. It really elevates the experience and makes the upgrade feel worthwhile.

3) When I caught The Predator at Premiere 2 hall, the sound was loud, immersive & clear. Exactly what I expect from a premium hall. Sadly wasn’t the case for Premiere 1 hall.

Areas of Improvement:
1) The lighting for the seat numbers is very bright in Premiere 1 hall (refer to photo) and was distracting during the movie. Dimming them slightly would improve the overall ambience.

2) Because the team is new, I understand they’re still adapting but there were noticeable inconsistencies:
- Premiere 2 hall, I was given cutlery and tissue with my pizza. Premiere 1 hall, I wasn’t.
- I encountered issues with both Premiere halls: once I couldn’t use the discount code and another time my order didn’t arrive until staff repeatedly came back and forth to resolve it.
The staff are young and clearly trying their best but the service processes may need refinement so the experience feels smooth and premium.

3) When I watched Wicked in Premiere 1, the audio sounded muffled and soft, especially from the front speakers. This was quite noticeable and very different from my excellent experience in Premiere 2. I’m not sure if this was due to the sound mix provided for the film or the hall setup but the inconsistency between the two halls was obvious.

Despite the hiccups, the improvements to the cinema are significant & I appreciate having such a comfortable well-designed place close to home. Just need adjustments to service consistency and technical setup (JEM please don’t disappoint Shaw good sound system reputation).

Thank you.

Very clean and movie quality very clear and good!👍🏻✨
Cinema here is new so come check it out while it's still very new can clean🤣🤣
Overall very nice, staffs also very friendly. Bought tickets through machine easy to use.
👍🏻✨👍🏻👍🏻✨✨👍🏻✨👍🏻👍🏻✨

my first time at shaw theatres jem! very clean and the seats were really comfortable
i was surprised by the sound system here, it’s really really good, even for a normal digital screening! out of the three times i’ve watch project hail mary this was by far the best experience
5 stars 🤩

first time coming to jem for a premium movie and it was a good experience. we only got fries but i was surprised they had ice creams and alcohol available too. the whole atmosphere is calm with dim lighting, plush seats and tons of legroom. it’s super family friendly but still feels grown up so you can actually relax. kamalii gave us a quick tour and explained all about the premium options so we knew what we were getting. the premium waiting area looks excellent and the service was really good, way better than the other outlets i’ve been to. she made us feel like vips, not just another booking. we’re definitely coming back here soon.

Just a road across from Jurong Point, Boon Lay Interchange and Boon Lay MRT. The Frontier CC is quite big and fully air-conditioned. There is a library, Yamaha music school and tuition/learning centers in the CC building.

Very convenient, can direct access from Jurong Point shopping mall through the flyover link. You can book the indoor sport court for badminton
Community space that combined library and services. Nice and clean place. Always go there for activities.

Cc has banned ANY types of PMA into the cc. Even a disabled person who urgently needs to use a toilet but has difficulties walking has to park OUTSIDE on the pedestrian pavement and walk in. The security guards are also very unprofessional and are against disabled people.

We brought our little one to the KidSperience @ Boon Lay event 12 Oct 2025, and it was such a meaningful and fun-filled day! ❤️

The kids had a great time exploring different careers — from doctor and banker to firefighter and more — through hands-on role play. It was amazing to see their excitement and curiosity as they learned and imagined what they could be in the future! 🌟

A big thank you to Boon Lay Community Centre and everyone involved for organizing such a wonderful event. The effort, creativity, and attention to detail truly showed, and we’re so grateful for the unforgettable experience. 🙏

Hope to see more of these amazing events in the future — they’re such great opportunities for children to learn, grow, and most importantly, have fun! 💕
Cashier is friendly and polite. Serve me well and fast respond in my order. Best place to dine in or take away. Great service.

I went in to level 2 and level 3 of the CC. there are a lot of enclose room even the door see thru portion are blocked with papers . Highly suspicious rooms are use for other purpose . The authority should audit those rooms 😂

Pizza hut is here and several ATMs, basketball court, study room, multi purpose hall that can turn into badminton court and a nice auditorium. They usually arrange nice programs for the boon lay residents. Near by plenty of food courts. St Francis of Assisi church is next to it.

nice openning field, good for jogging and exercising, there are few barbecue roof but needs to book in advanced, there is a kid playing field also. few constructions sites nearby are being in progress, not sure if it’s still spacy like this in the future.

From walking the isle of trees shadowing above us to getting to the playground towering up high for kids to try. Big enough space to cycle and do things freely however the slides needs to be repaired to enjoy maximum enjoyment.

Quiet and serene place.
There is a senior citizen workout area, couples of playground, couples of bbq pit, a street soccer court, shelther tentages etc. Great for family gathering, workout enthu
I love this park but it's full of inappropriate drawings but that does not affect me. I hope one day the workers will do something about it

Spacious Barbeque area, lot of facilities for children activities, Jogging track, cycling track, Exercise equipments for all ages and with wheel chair access and can do exercises without need to move out of wheel chair. Nice Breeze in the evening. Well maintained place.👍

🌿Choa Chu Kang Park is situated opposite Choa Chu Kang Bus Interchange along Choa Chu Kang Drive.
🚶‍➡️🏃‍➡️It's a community garden within the Choa Chu Kang Estate where residents gather for morning workout, walk, jog and do some gardening.
🌿This green oasis offers a relief to the hectic city pace.
🛝🐕‍🦺🍆Like many community gardens, CCK Park too have its fair share of Playground, Fitness Corners, Dog Run, Allotment Garden, Open Lawn and Amphitheatre.
🛼🛹🌶️Besides these regular features, CCK Park have a Skate Plaza and a Community Garden.
🍂Also, somewhat hidden from view, is an Old Tomb Trail.
🚍How to get here:
Exit from Choa Chu Kang Bus Interchange to Choa Chu Kang Drive.
Cross over the road.
Park is next to Shell Station.
Some parts under remodeling.  Over all still good for visiting. Quite, shady with Macedonia just outside SAFRA, and walking distance to  Lot 1 and CCK MRT.

The park is clean, well-maintained, and very peaceful. It has lush green lawns, shaded walking paths, and plenty of seating areas. The atmosphere is refreshing and ideal for morning walks, yoga, or spending relaxed time with family and children. Overall, it’s a great place for fitness, leisure, and connecting with nature.

Can an urban park be a space for relaxation, education and creativity? Can it become a unique attraction of the region? Or maybe you are looking for a business idea and want to create an outdoor eductation center? Bet on an interactive park of experiences!

Families are increasingly looking for unusual forms of recreation. When traveling on a trip, they want to not only visit the beautiful old town or museum, but also to provide their children interesting experiences, combined with education. Experience Park is a place where children will not be bored!

The open-air museum will provide great entertainment and learning through play. You can touch each exhibit, run it and learn about the laws of nature through the individual experimentation.

Until now, city parks were equipped with benches, fountains, and nature monuments. Recently, you can also work out in the outdoor gym. It is worth to equip this space with educational devices and in addition to muscle training, practice the mind as well! We suggest creating an educational path, which may consist of several thematic zones, located in different parts of the park.

Small park with lots of ants, wouldnt recommend to come for a stroll either day/night. Most people come here for Mr Teh Tarik Cartel that's located inside the park itself (this would definitely recommend).

​The park's main activities are conveniently clustered around McDonald's, which serves as a great central spot for dining, socializing, and recreation. Aside from active play, it is a fantastic place for gatherings, featuring camping grounds, barbecue pits, large open lawns for picnics, kite flying and jogging.

West Coast Park is a long but relatively quiet park that stretches along Singapore’s western coastline. It’s a wonderful place for a relaxed outdoor day, especially for families with kids

One of the highlights is the large playground, which is one of the better ones in Singapore. The climbing structures, slides, and play areas keep children entertained for a long time, and there’s plenty of open space nearby for running around

The park is also great for cycling and casual walks. You can easily rent bicycles or family pedal carts near the entrance and ride along the park connectors while enjoying the sea breeze

For those who enjoy spending more time outdoors, the park has BBQ pits and camping areas, making it perfect for weekend gatherings with friends and family. There are also scenic spots along the waterfront where people fish or simply relax and watch the ships in the distance

I do like to come to this nature park by the sea - to take in the salty air and the cool sea breeze, both of which relaxes and then invigorates me. I like to hear the waves crashing against the shore and to be among the greenery in this nature park - in particular, the bamboo fringed walkway as regards the latter. The amenities at this nature park is adequate - with enough car parking spaces and public toilets. And overall, this nature park is well-maintained.
Well maintained park and a good place to spend time alone or with family and friends. Suitable for all ages, whether to exercise in the mornings or catch a lovely sunset in the evenings.

West Coast Park is considerably smaller than East Coast Park (ECP), but it remains a highly viable zone for R&R. I have scouted the perimeter and identified several optimal coordinates with good sightlines to hold position and decompress.

Civilian population density in this grid is minimal. The decreased foot traffic results in a secure perimeter and a highly tranquil atmosphere—ideal for lowering your guard and recovering stamina.

Be advised on two minor variables:

First, visuals on the maritime border are compromised and do not match the aesthetics of ECP.

Second, heavy transport vessels are active in the adjacent waters. My MJOLNIR armor’s environmental sensors occasionally detected industrial exhaust drifting into the area. It is non-lethal, but noticeable without a helmet filtration system.

Overall assessment: A solid, quiet sector for downtime. Proceed to coordinates at your discretion.

Sungei Buloh Wetland Reserve is one of the best places in Singapore to experience nature in its most authentic form. From the moment I entered via the Neo Tiew Crescent entrance, I was surrounded by lush greenery, peaceful trails, and the soothing sounds of wildlife

The reserve is exceptionally well-maintained, with clear signage, wide boardwalks, and excellent visitor facilities at the Wetland Centre. The elevated wooden walkways make it easy to explore the mangrove forests while enjoying beautiful views of the surrounding wetlands

One of the highlights of my visit was observing mudskippers hopping across the mudflats among the mangrove roots. It was fascinating to see these unique creatures thriving in their natural habitat. The reserve offers plenty of opportunities to spot wildlife, so keep an eye out for monitor lizards, crabs, and migratory birds

I was also impressed by the Mudskipper Pod, a beautifully designed wooden observation structure that blends seamlessly into the environment. It provides a cool and shaded resting point with panoramic views over the mangroves and the Johor Strait. The architecture adds a unique touch to the overall experience without detracting from the natural surroundings

A refreshing escape into nature! We enjoyed a slow and peaceful walk at Sungei Buloh Wetland Reserve on May 27, fully immersed in the calm beauty of the wetlands. The walking paths were beautifully lined with lush trees, creating a naturally shaded and cooling environment that made the stroll especially comfortable even during the warmer hours.
The tranquil surroundings, gentle sounds of nature, and fresh air provided a relaxing break from the busy city atmosphere. Along the way, we appreciated the well-maintained trails and the opportunity to observe the rich biodiversity and greenery up close.
It is a wonderful place for a leisurely walk, nature appreciation, photography, or simply to unwind and recharge. Highly recommended for anyone looking to enjoy a peaceful and scenic outdoor experience in Singapore.

A beautiful and peaceful escape into nature! The mangroves, fresh air, and rich wildlife make every walk feel special. Perfect for a relaxing stroll or bird watching—especially in the early morning or evening.
Stay curious and keep your eyes open… you might spot a giant monitor lizard crossing the path or even a crocodile resting in the water. A little adventure awaits you here!

My family took a late afternoon walk down to Sungei Buloh during low tide today to unwind, and it was a great way to clear our heads. The air had that familiar, heavy scent of salt and damp mangrove mud, and with the water stripped back, the exposed api-api roots and glistening mudflats completely changed the landscape. We spent a while just watching the giant mudskippers drag themselves across the silt and bicker over territory, which is always entertaining. The low water also meant the local birds were out in full force. We managed to spot a Great Egret freezing in place before spearing a small fish, and a Grey Heron standing completely still in the shallows waiting for Archerfish. Finishing up on the main bridge during the golden hour was the highlight, looking down into the shallow river channel at schools of Halfbeaks skimming the surface under the evening sky was incredibly peaceful and a solid reminder of how nice it is to have this kind of wilderness right on our doorstep.
Really nice nature walk. There is one bus that goes there. Can spend about 3-4 hours there. You get to see the swamps, facing JB, lizards, fishes, crabs, birds etc.

Follow the trail to Jelutong Tower and Tree Top Walk. Enjoy the view and the trail. Have not cover all the trails. Will be back to try and experience other trails. Good experience to spend weekends with nature. Escape from the city view.

This area consist of a network of jungle trails where you can trek. There many tracks that you can walk and the natural forest canopy provides a wonderful refreshing air with minimal pollution. Many kind of flora that you can enjoy along the way. You can track anyway between 45 mins to over 4 hours.

The place is serene! Most of the paths are manmade now so it's easier to hike up to tree top walk! There are A LOT of monkeys do keep your loose items safe at all times, also multiple signs are put up to not feed the monkeys.

speechlessly beautiful. just yeah. the biggest nature reserve in sg.

ps there’s a lot of monkeys so pls don’t bring plastic bags, they’re very aggressive as well
Nice place for walking and jogging. The trails are well marked. Great nature!!!

Love this newly renovated Jurong Eco-Garden Park. The greenery is lush and well-maintained, with a beautiful man-made lake that adds to the peaceful atmosphere. The best time to visit is early morning or late evening. Do remember to bring sunscreen and mosquito repellent.

Be cautious of wild boars in the area, and watch your step on rocky or gravel paths, as they can be slippery or uneven. There are also some fallen branches along shaded areas that may be easy to miss, so take care while walking.

The lake is a great spot for wedding or photography shoots. You might even spot a giant turtle—likely a Southern River Terrapin—alongside common terrapins. It’s huge and almost looks like a monster! Tilapia are abundant here, and kids will enjoy feeding the fish (bring some bread).

Do note that fishing is not allowed. The toilets are clean, and there’s a water filter station available along the walking path. Overall, a lovely place to relax and enjoy nature.

Very nice garden/park! Good for casual running. Has fitness equipment and playgrounds. Has a water cooler. There are toilets and an additional toilet for individuals with limited accessibility. Lots of parking lots as well. Best timings to come are in the morning around 7-9am and in the evening 6.30-7.30pm.

A park just opposite my house, via a bridge over PIE. Quiet as little people, you can hear the birds chipping as you walk along. Playground for children, toilet available. Visit the pottery and saw lovey chickens. There are pottery for sale like huge vases, plates, cups, etc. You can walk to the campus for food, not sure if open on weekend.

It’s a very new park with lots of eco features. I’ll say that it’s actually an exhibition of water technologies used by the semi-public companies in the area and what you’re actually seeing is the experimental site where they’re showcasing the results and testing their ideas irl. Quite cool actually. BUT ALOT OF MOSQUITOES THAT WILL BITE AND STING YOU. I had itchy spots everywhere. (minus one star sorry - bugs can coexist with us but in harmony please! why the Wolbachia not working issit)

Finally the garden is open to public, nice garden for a run or cycling. There is a playground / fitness corner / toilet / drinking water and a big pond. The place is of average size. Nice little place to chill after my run.

🌧️Jurong Eco Garden (The Potter's Garden) Eco Pond together with the Freshwater Swamp holds much of the rainwater around the area.
🚿These water are then used as non-portable water for various uses around the Garden.
⛲Before it is used, the water undergo aeration and cleansing process by natural filteration to remove excessive nutrients, enhance the water quality.
🦅🐢🐟The entire Eco Pond and Freshwater Swamp Forest support a diverse ecosystem including aquatic and other form of wildlifes

Peaceful & breezy. One of the historical places.

I hiked all the way in, and it was peaceful and quiet.
Very good place to learn history from the boards.

Omg such a really nice place to visit. Its classy. The building ng has all the luxury stores from a to z worth millions of dollars. Very neatly maintained. Great tourist spot. Highly recommended place for everyone.

I’ve been there many times and I still never get bored. I love the vibe, whenever I visit Singapore, I know I’ll be walking a lot

It’s quite similar to Jakarta in terms of public transport, everything connects to tourist attractions and busy areas, but Singapore is way more organized

People and vehicles are very disciplined, there’s traffic sometimes, but not as chaotic as Jakarta LOL

Honestly, I’ll probably never get tired of going to Singapore...

Marina Bay Waterfront is easily one of the most breathtaking places to visit in Singapore. The moment you arrive, you’re greeted by a stunning panoramic view of the city skyline, with iconic skyscrapers rising elegantly against the sky, especially magical as the sun begins to set and the city lights slowly come alive.

The promenade itself is impeccably clean and well maintained, making it a pleasure to walk along. Every step feels comfortable and safe, whether you’re taking a casual stroll, jogging, or simply enjoying the fresh air. Along the way, you’ll find a wide variety of restaurants and cafés, offering everything from quick bites to fine dining with incredible views.

What truly stands out is how many beautiful photo spots are scattered throughout the area. Whether it’s the skyline reflection on the water, modern architectural landmarks, or perfectly landscaped corners, it’s a paradise for photography lovers.

There’s also a spacious garden area where you can sit back, relax, and soak in the atmosphere.

Overall, Marina Bay Waterfront perfectly blends urban sophistication with natural beauty. It’s a must-visit spot whether you’re a tourist or a local looking for a peaceful yet visually stunning escape

Marina Bay Waterfront Promenade -
A Symphony of Light, Architecture, and Heartfelt Memories: An Unforgettable Experience at Marina Bay Waterfront Promenade

My recent visit to Singapore was filled with incredible sights, but nothing captured the soul of this modern city-state quite like my evenings spent walking along the Marina Bay Waterfront Promenade. It is not merely a path; it is an experience, a masterpiece of urban design, and a testament to Singapore’s vision of blending nature with concrete

. I am writing this with immense gratitude to the planners, maintenance teams, and the city of Singapore for maintaining such a world-class, clean, and awe-inspiring space.

The First Impression: A Grand Promenade
From the moment I stepped onto the wide, impeccably maintained promenade, I was struck by the shear scale and beauty of the area. Stretching 3.5 kilometers around the bay, the promenade serves as a "grand front porch", linking major attractions like the Helix Bridge, Marina Bay Sands, and the Esplanade—Theatres on the Bay.

The attention to detail is remarkable; the flooring is comfortable for long walks, and there is a perfect balance between open spaces and shaded areas to escape the tropical heat.
Breathtaking Views and Architectural Marvels

The panoramic views from the promenade are absolutely surreal. Walking along the water, I felt surrounded by architectural masterpieces. On one side, the iconic Marina Bay Sands hotel towers with its awe-inspiring rooftop Skypark, while on the other, the futuristic dome of the ArtScience Museum resembles a lotus flower opening, perfectly framed by the skyline of the Central Business District.

I highly recommend visiting in the late afternoon, as I did. It allowed me to see the skyline transition from the bright, sunny afternoon into the "golden hour" before dusk, when the city lights begin to shimmer. The way the lights from the skyscrapers reflect on the calm water is a sight I will treasure forever—it’s like a postcard come to life.

The Magic of the Evening: Light, Sound, and Atmosphere
As the sun sets, the promenade transforms into a lively yet tranquil oasis. The atmosphere is vibrant, filled with people laughing, running, and capturing photos. I was particularly impressed by how the atmosphere changes from the bustling, lively sections near the dining options to the more serene, reflective stretches.

A heartfelt thank you to the organizers of the Spectra Light and Water Show. Watching the laser lights and dancing fountains projected to music right in front of the Shoppes at Marina Bay Sands was a highlight of my trip. It is a stunning, free, and accessible experience that showcases the city's commitment to public enjoyment.
A Sensory Experience: Mist Walk and Greenery

The promenade is designed with thoughtful features that make the walk incredibly enjoyable. I specifically loved the Mist Walk—a 250-meter long metal structure that emits a refreshing mist of water. It was a wonderful, creative way to cool down while strolling along the waterfront. Furthermore, the landscaping, with various trees and flowering shrubs, provides a natural feel that softens the surrounding modern skyline.

I visited Marina Bay Sands around golden hour and it was an amazing experience. The warm sunlight on the buildings and the reflection on the bay made the whole place look magical. The skyline view is incredible and the area is perfect for relaxing, walking, and taking photos.
Great track for a simple workout.  Peaceful and very empty.  7 to 8km from Mandai Rd to Chestnut Nature Park.

relatively straightforward trail that stretches from Mandai to Chestnut Nature Park. not crowded compared to the more popular ones; ohhh have to share the path with mountain bikers so do keep a lookout for them and generally just keep to one side (I stay on the left) (there are some sections that are marked "for biking only" though). certain sections are right next to the expressway... tranquility is so near yet so far away ;)

A late morning hike started from Zhenghua Nature Park via Central Catchment Nature Reserve / Mandai T15 Trail, ended at Mandai Wildlife Reserve 👍

Initially, wanted to explore the Mandai Boardwalk. However, the afternoon sun was scorching hot!! Thus, ends after a 2-hour hike. I will explore the SG Zoo, bird park (yet to visit since the relocation) next time!

Overall, it’s a pleasant hike ~spotted another squirrel 🐿️

Ps. There’s NO public toilet 🚾 and water cooler on this trail!!

An easy trail off Mandai Road, next to the Zoo and the Bird Paradise. It is a no frills trail with no shelter, washrooms, significant landmarks nor sights along the way. The path was blocked about half way through by a fallen tree. It was not difficult to climb over the fallen tree but may be a little more challenging for cyclists who need to carry their bikes over the blockage. The entire trail took less than 1.5 hours to reach Chestnut Nature Park from Mandai Road.

Look out for the mountain bikes bikers at the start of the trail down to chestnut. Bikers are usually very nice and generous with their morning greetings :) important to look down at where you are walking, that would cause a bit of a neck ache. All is well for the second half of the trail when the routes are separated for hikers and bikers.

Ample space for everyone. Great for exercise. Kids of different ages can enjoy the wide variety of play areas.
However, some bigger kids play badminton and soccer near the playground areas where they endanger the other kids

a nice park connector, perfectly convenient for those in the area! this is an ideal place for taking a nice stroll or to cycle. it is also a fork of connection between those living in the area and the bus stop at this connector, making travelling enjoyable and convenient. this is definitely a nice park!
Been cycling this area quite sometime, and look what I've found at the canal, surprisingly delightful.
nice place for morning or evening jog and walk. can get kinda crowded on weekends. when the river water is substantial, no foul smell, else sometimes quite pungent ..
Awesome view along whampoa rivee

place_name: Raffles Kidz @ Jurong West (Surbana Jurong Campus) – Best Childcare Centre and Preschool
review_text:
My 4 year old daughter just got transferred to Raffleskidz school this mid year and I so happy that I made the perfect choice for her.
The staff has made it a very conducive environment for her to thrive. Before Raffleskidz, she couldn’t recognize the letters and numbers. Within a few weeks, I could see great improvement in her academic. The teachers conducted themselves very professionally and always provide updates and photos of her progress. I’m proud to say that she’s a RafflesKidz !
And a special shoutout to Teacher Anna and 李老师! Thanks for all the TLC !

place_name: Raffles Kidz @ Jurong West (Surbana Jurong Campus) – Best Childcare Centre and Preschool
review_text:
Our child has been at this preschool for the past 1.5 years, and one thing we’ve really valued is the stability of the team. The teachers and principal — as well as the cook and cleaner — have been a consistent presence throughout, which has made such a difference for both children and parents. Communication is always easy and responsive, and most importantly, our toddler genuinely likes and trusts them and usually looks forward to going to school each day.

The teachers are attentive, caring, and seem genuinely happy to be there. It’s clear they truly care for the children, and we feel completely comfortable entrusting our child to them.

We also love the school environment itself. The classrooms are bright and welcoming, with lots of natural light from the floor-to-ceiling windows. The children are regularly brought out of the classrooms, and the larger campus is a huge plus — indoor trees, greenery, and airy shared spaces mean the kids still get exposure to light and nature while remaining in a comfortable air-conditioned environment, even on very hot or rainy days.

Would definitely recommend this preschool to families looking for a nurturing and well-run preschool in the far west.

place_name: Raffles Kidz @ Jurong West (Surbana Jurong Campus) – Best Childcare Centre and Preschool
review_text:
A great school with modern facilities located in a modern building. If you're thinking of enrolling your kids here:

- Has underground parking and lift access directly in front of the school, removing any inconveniences caused by the weather.

- The school shares a very large common area which stretches the length of the building. It is sheltered and air-conditioned throughout if you wish to hang around for awhile after picking up your child.

- The school uses the montessori approach as the method of teaching which focuses on independence and natural desire to learn.

- It is a premium and highly-regarded school with humble principal and teachers who never hesitate to answer our questions about our child's day with them.

It is admittedly expensive but as parents, we are assured we are providing the best education for our child.

place_name: Raffles Kidz @ Jurong West (Surbana Jurong Campus) – Best Childcare Centre and Preschool
review_text:
I couldn't be happier with my experience at Raffles Kidz International in Surbana Jurong! My 21-month-old son started here in March 2024, and it's been amazing to see his growth and development in such a short time.

The teachers, especially Teacher Nasihah and Guo Laoshi, are incredibly caring and supportive. They made my son's transition into school life smooth and enjoyable by sending regular picture updates during the first few weeks. I've noticed  positive changes in his behavior at home like putting away his water bottles and greeting people in the lift/playground, which is a testament to the nurturing environment they provide.

The school staff are also efficient and proactive in communicating school events, ensuring that parents are always in the loop . The school provides ample space for learning and exploration, just that it's a little out of the way. It's better to drive as the nearest bus stop (no.199) can be quite some distance away. Anyway, there is a LRT station currently under construction near the school.

Overall, I highly recommend Raffles Kidz International to any parent looking for a nurturing and educational environment for their child. Huge thanks to the entire team !

place_name: Raffles Kidz @ Jurong West (Surbana Jurong Campus) – Best Childcare Centre and Preschool
review_text: We feel good dropping off our son at school knowing he is in safe and caring hands. Many thanks to my son's teachers (Teacher Anna, Tang Lao Shi and Teacher Su), other teachers and the school's non-teaching staff  for playing a part in creating a positive environment for the children. Highly recommend the school especially while it's still so new and has low teacher-student ratios :)

Ace @ Work Cashew Heights was our child’s first preschool after relocating back to Singapore, and it became a very meaningful starting point for us as a family.

We’re especially grateful to Ms Iris and the team (special thanks to Ms Mara, Ms Janina, Ms Sharlene and Chen Lao shi) for their patience, care, and dedication. Our child had a difficult time settling in at the beginning, and they handled it with so much understanding and consistency. It made a big difference to us, knowing he was in a safe and supportive environment.

The preschool may be small, but it has a strong sense of community. Over time, we got to know not just the teachers, but also the other children and their families. It really has a warm “kampung” kind of feel that’s hard to find.

We’re moving to another part of the island, so it’s time for us to say goodbye. We’ll miss everyone ;,))

Thank you again to the Ace@Work team for being part of this chapter with us.🫶🏻

Our daughter had a great time learning and socializing with her friends and teachers at Ace. She was particularly attached to the principal, Ms Iris, who is simply amazing in every way. Special thanks to Ms Shimin and Dong Laoshi for their care and dedication in managing a class of young and demanding toddlers. We highly recommend this centre and were sad to leave when the family had to relocate.

I highly recommend this childcare center! It is situated within a secure condominium, providing a safe environment. My children love playing in the playground after school. The principal, Ms. Iris, is incredibly friendly and communicates with us effortlessly, offering great support. The teachers are all cheerful and clearly passionate about working with children. A special thanks to Ms. Dong Laoshi, Ms. Zhang Laoshi, Ms. Shimin, and Ms. Janina for their dedication. The curriculum is well-structured, and my child enjoys the enrichment activities, including speech and drama and ping pong. Both of my children truly love this childcare center!

My son is visibly happier in this school. When he accidentally wet himself, teacher Pat chose very kind words to remind him to tell her in future if he needs to pee. In a previous school. The teacher would scold him and pressure us parents to either toilet train him asap or put him back on diapers. The choice of words has a significant impact on a child's mind and confidence. ACE  @ Cashew Crescent has done a fantastic job of this and we are grateful.

Post 1 year update.
I enrolled my daughter in this school as well. I believe her teachers Ms Jing Xiao Qian and Miss Nicia have guided Sophia to being herself and allowing her talents to come alive. Miss Jing and Miss Nicia are truly dedicated to bringing out the best not just in my kids but in the whole class as well. They spent countless hours in making all these beautiful decorations for Sophia's graduation ceremony and making an unforgettable experience for the children and parents.

I should state that I write this as a typical young parent with the same stresses as every other young parent and I can tell u that Miss Jing , Miss Nicia , all ACE teachers are very well led by the principal Miss Iris Lee. They focus on building confidence in the kids and guiding them to be themselves.

I have first hand experience sending my kids to My First Skool and I boldy state that I regret sending my kids to My First Skool. The teachers I encountered were robotic and dogmatic to the point of criticizing and almost bullying children who lag behind. After sending my kids to ACE @ Cashew. I observed a big difference in my children. They are happier and more willing to be themselves and imagine without boundaries. I believe Ms Iris Lee, Miss Jing and Miss Nicia and all the other ACE teachers have played an instrumental role in allowing this to happen.

I am writing this review more to express my gratitude to this wonderful group of teachers and share my experience with other young parents.

The teachers provide a fun and enjoyable learning experience for the kids. Especially Jing Laoshi, Ms Nicia, Han Laoshi and Ms Iris. They are always planning enriching activities for the children. They take great effort in organising events that parents can take part too. Their hard work and heart work can be seen in the K2 graduation concert where they patiently guided the children for months to put up an amazing performance. Thank you teachers and staff at Ace@Work Cashew Heights!

Overall, Millennia Institute serves as a valuable alternative pathway to the A-Levels. It is particularly suitable for students who prefer a steadier academic pace, appreciate a supportive environment, and are willing to take ownership of their learning over a longer period.

the exam papers are so easy that they provide a sense of security for students, not preparing them for the actual examination ahead.

If you wanted someone to fulfil just that segment of the event, you could just record that ‘outstanding student’s speech and play it during the event. Shouldn’t have wasted my time.

Beautiful big campus. Can be quite a walk to the various schools. The canteen near the health sciences zone is very spacious, and quite a few international cuisines on offer. Lucky staff and students.
The staff and students at the optometry dept were all polite and professional. The Facility looked like a very good place to groom future optometrist.

​Ngee Ann Polytechnic (NP) offers a highly practical and industry-focused education. The curriculum is regularly updated with input from industry partners, and lecturers, often with professional experience, provide a realistic and engaging learning environment. The focus on project-based learning encourages critical thinking and problem-solving, which I found to be a more effective method of learning than traditional memorization.

​While the polytechnic's modular system can be demanding, with a constant cycle of assignments and projects, it effectively prepares students for the pressures of both higher education and the workplace. The campus life is equally vibrant, with a wide array of CCAs, excellent facilities, and diverse food options.

​Overall, NP provides a well-rounded experience, blending academic rigor with a rich student life. It's a great choice for students who thrive in a hands-on environment and are seeking to build a strong foundation for their future careers.

It sounds like you're planning a trip to Ngee Ann Polytechnic (NP) Block 23! Here's a quick rundown to help you get there:

1. Take the MRT to Clementi Station.
2. At Clementi Station, exit towards Bus Interchange.
3. Board Bus 154 from Clementi Bus Interchange.
4. Get off at Ngee Ann Polytechnic (NP) stop.

You can use Google Maps or a transit app like Grab or Moovit for the most up-to-date bus schedules and routes. Have a smooth ride to your company event! 🚍🎉..

Ngee Ann Polytechnic has this warm, almost “campus village” vibe. The greenery is everywhere — huge trees, long slopes, and shaded areas that make the place feel peaceful even when there are a lot of students around. NP is known for being very student-focused, and you can actually feel that when walking through the school. There are tons of small places to sit down and study, from open-air benches to indoor lounges.

The campus spirit is strong. A lot of students seem genuinely happy to be there — you’ll see groups practising dance, people playing guitar, students sitting around chatting after lessons, and clubs actively doing their thing. The Co-Curricular culture is particularly strong; NP is one of the polys where the arts and student life are very vibrant.

What I liked:

Extremely beautiful surroundings; feels like studying in a nature park sometimes.

Many programmes are well-known and respected, especially mass comm and business.

Good social vibes — friendly atmosphere and active clubs everywhere.

Lots of cozy corners to study, hang out, or relax between classes.

What could be better:

The slopes are legendary — going up and down the hills every day becomes a workout.

Classrooms and older blocks are slightly dated in some areas.

Some canteens fill up very quickly, especially during lunch hours.

Overall:
NP feels like a warm, welcoming campus with plenty of life. If you like greenery, community feeling, and a comfortable environment, this is a great place.

I'm here waiting for my friend and my first time here. So natural greeny, relaxing and peaceful. Facilities here clean, stable and comfy ambience. Love it here. Would recommend to all friend and family.

math since third grade. Since Mr. Tok helped him with math, his grades have improved to a passing level. I am very happy.
Thanks for the effort to improve the math and science strength. she was received good practice materials from the center, I will recommend to friends this centre for the tution.

He would contact me immediately when i text me at any time . He  had guided my daughter very closely throughout the year . Finally about the scores, my daughter have improved significantly, AL 6 to AL 4 in prelims in both math and science

Mr Andrew is an excellent tutor who found many different ways to keep the lessons fun, engaging and informative using lots of different animations. His teaching style helped improve my grades in math and science a bunch. So grateful for his help!!

Teacher Andrew is a great teacher who makes Science interesting. He also cares about the students wellbeing too. After studying there for 6 years, I achieved a A2 in the GCE O level Examinations. Thanks Teacher Andrew for being patient and teaching me well!

Andrew is an exceptional tutor. He is engaging and explains math and scientific concepts clearly, helping my daughter build real confidence. My daughter enjoys the lessons and has made noticeable improvements. Highly recommended!

Teacher Andrew is an amazing maths tutor! Before I started lessons with him, I found some maths topics really confusing and wasn’t very confident in exams. He explains everything in a really clear and simple way, and he’s very patient when going through questions step by step.
His lessons helped me understand the concepts much better. He also shared helpful tips on how to approach different types of questions.Thanks to his guidance and support, I was able to improve a lot and got an A in Maths! Teacher Andrew is very knowledgeable and explains difficult topics in a simple and easy-to-understand way. My son’s grades improved and scored A in O level ,he is also very responsible and always well-prepared for lessons.

A place to study. Good air con. Open to NTU people. Lighting still ok. A lot of knowledge here but none knows how to apply or how it works. Ever studying but also brain dead at the same time.
