Take a break Students.

Lee Wee Nam Library at NTU is hands-down one of the best places on campus to get stuff done. Whether you're cramming for finals, working on a group project, or just need a quiet place to chill with your laptop, this library’s got you covered.

The space is bright, modern, and surprisingly cozy for a university library. There’s a mix of seating — quiet study zones, comfy lounge chairs, and group discussion rooms if you need to collaborate. The views from the windows are also a nice bonus, especially if you snag a seat by the glass.

Tech-wise, it's well-equipped with plenty of power sockets, printers, and fast Wi-Fi. The online resources and book selection are solid, especially if you're in engineering or tech — not surprising, given it's right next to the School of Computer Science and Engineering.

It can get pretty packed during exam season, so go early if you want a good spot. But overall, it’s one of the more comfortable and efficient places to study on campus.

The study area on Level 5 is generally quiet and comfortable, making it a nice spot to study. However, the air conditioning is not very strong, which can make it quite warm and uncomfortable at times, especially if you're seated near the windows.

Went to the library as a visitor, not a student. But I was with a group of friends, and one of them is an NTU student. He asked if we could hang around the library as visitors. The girl who was in charge on Saturday 27/7/24 around 4 to 5pm was being very rude. She was extremely unfriendly. We were being as quiet as a mouse. But she was being very strict that we should only hang around the entrance area. This is understandable, however she could have been polite and friendly. What a fierce tigress! 4 stars only because the library environment was pretty nice and cozy. Zero star to that person in charge.
The library looks great but some spots lack AC.

place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text:
Visited the National Library Building mainly for The Albatross File: Singapore’s Independence Declassified exhibition, and it turned out to be a meaningful experience. The exhibition itself is relatively small, but the historical significance is immense. It provides a fascinating and candid look into the events leading up to Singapore’s separation from Malaysia in 1965, including documents and notes showing that both sides had, by then, come to see separation as the likely path forward. Seeing the actual letters, transcripts, and handwritten notes made this pivotal chapter of Singapore’s history feel very real and personal

I also took some time to roam around the Lee Kong Chian Reference Library and was impressed by how incredibly well managed the entire place is. The building itself is architecturally striking, modern, spacious, and filled with natural light. The library environment feels calm, organized, and conducive for learning and research, with clear signage and well-maintained facilities throughout

place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text: The National Library is conveniently located with good traffic access from around the island. It’s a comfy cool place to be if your want to study, research and just reading. You may spent hours but no worries about hunger as the are many choices of food in the area.

place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text:
2026 January - Did quick look around, enjoyed reading the stories from the mini exhibition Knots of Time. Looks like there are other exhibitions and all sorts of collection, will be back to explore and read.

Great ambience, nice for researchers and the public alike. Place has plenty of space if you'd like to get some work done, offers brilliant views, and even has a skygarden. Power and water is readily available, and theres even sufficient space for a stroll if you're feeling a mental block coming on.

Will be back to check out the albatross files. The atrium at the bottom is very pleasant, and the vaguely 'heritage' old chang kee seems like a good place to check out as well.

place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text: The Albatross File exhibition at NLB is a fascinating showcase of Singapore’s separation history, featuring declassified documents, oral histories, and immersive multimedia displays. It brings to life the tense years leading up to independence, blending archival records with dramatized storytelling.

Albatross exhibition of Singapore

Thankful for this curated history that bares all that Singaporeans may not know. We had the privilege of having seniors in their 70s who came along, cementing our understanding of what they had gone thru.

The whole setup allowed us to go at our own pace and the voice of our late and retired ministers add a sense of urgency and realism.

A suggestion for those who are not English educated, as the ministers are speaking in English, please allow translator set made avail in various language to our non English speaking seniors.

Lastly the wooden board is super heavy. Made it very difficult for us to lift up the board to read the files.  Lastly is there a need to have the board hidden? Most don’t know it exist until someone lifted it up.

This has to be one of the worst bus interchanges I have experienced.

Bus timings are consistently slow, especially for service 285. The intervals are too long, and queues often stretch all the way to the automatic entrance doors. It is not uncommon to see large crowds waiting.

When the bus finally comes, it is usually already packed. There have been occasions where it skips the stop entirely because no more passengers can board. This makes the commute unpredictable and unnecessarily stressful.

Because of these delays and poor intervals, I have been late on multiple occasions. Something needs to be improved, whether it is frequency or crowd management.

Bus 232 to Clementi West is at least 13 minutes per bus. So low frequency and waiting time is long! Although they have multiple buses, but the interval time for other bus even higher (201@14min; and 175@18min). When 232 come, 201 and 175 also coming about same time. Meaning if you miss certain timepoint, you have to wait for additional 10plus minutes for the next bus to come, even through they have 3 buses. You don't feel the convenient of having more option. 😩😩😩

I am honouring Clementi Bus Interchange for being home to Singapore's shortest bus route, Bus 284, which plies three bus stops along Clementi Avenue 4: Bus Stop no. 17211 (between Blocks 315/318), Bus Stop no. 17201 (Block 308) and Bus Stop no. 17219 (Block 376). The total distance covered is 1.8 kilometres long in one loop service. Unlike Woodlands' Bus 902 which ends at Republic Polytechnic, it is a unidirectional or one-way service with one single stop before terminating.

Clementi Bus Interchange is very conducive because of its medium-sized air-conditioned floor space. It is quite accessible and it does have plenty of entry and exit points to Clementi Mall. The arriving berths and departing berths are well-spaced to allow bus drivers ample driving and parking spaces at any time of the operational day.
The timing there is extremely inconsistent. Of all the places I’ve been to, the most disorganised, least punctual, and longest waiting times I’ve experienced were at Clementi Bus Interchange.
The place is not very big but really very convenience to let people go through to all directions.

It's a clean bus stop that works, but it also is in the middle of nowhere. If not for the fact that I'm visiting the line of restaurants near here, I honestly don't see myself needing to drop by here.
Not crowded. Scenic, clean park with a small strip of coastline. Includes a dog run, a pond, bar b queue pits, camping area etc.

Convenient for people to go and transfer train.
Clean.

This station is surrounded by the HDB block:) not much special things to see but it’s very quiet and. I’ve to walk around:) there’re some nice playgrounds inside the HDB near to this station:)

There are lots of food options here with affordable price. I personally like the western food, Japanese food and mixed veg rice here.
If you are first time here, recommend the western food for taste and price., the western comes with two side dishes and the portion is very generous. Taste is yummy!
The Japanese food is yummy, except the kimchi.
Cai peng tastes nice, very affordable and big portion.
The drink stall upstairs has a variety of choices.
The fish soup at level 1 is quite generous in terms of its portion, taste wise is average.
The duck rice is cheaper than other places, and portion is slightly bigger but the duck meat that I had is not tender tho, quite hard.
The wanton noodle upstairs is average, portion is small but has quite a special texture.
The noodle stall at level 1 (last pic) tastes bad, however, cheap, only $3 for bak cho Mee, not recommend unless you are tight in wallet. The dry bak cho Mee pork is so watery and the taste is so diluted therefore, noodle texture not good as well.
To conclude, would recommend this hawker centre here.

Found this very nice pan-fried pork bun @Xiangyu Pan-Fried Bun stall in the new Jurong West hawker center. Very hot & juicy inside. Nicer than xiao long bao.

Place is very spacious and clean and with basement carpark. Not crowded as place is new.

One of my favourite hawker centers! The seating is spacious and the fans make it cool. The food is always delicious, authentic and there are some unique stalls that serve up something different (and tasty)! My favourite go-to stall is the xiangju pan fried bun. It’s a bit like being in Shanghai, but with the Singaporean friendly touch! The rojak is a good comfort food alternative too.

1st tried at the decent stall serving freshly made beef noodles with generous portions. The broth is umami-rich and flavourful, yet light, not oily or overly salty. Hawker environment is clean & airy.

I tried the pan fried pork bun ($4.50 for 3 pcs) and nasi lemak ($3.50). They were both amazing. Really hearty and filling, also moderately spicy. I also tried their iced kopi ($1.50), which was strong and a perfect balance between sweet and nutty.

Really good food with spacious dining and multiple options. Reasonably priced as well. I would visit again to try other foods.

Disappointed with the air-flow, wind abit stale, not ventilating well since all the ceiling fans were operated during evening time. Cant feel much wind.

Hawker is spacious and clean, but services from specific stalls are confusing.
Firstly drink stall, my friend able to make his payment through paywave at the counter, but when my turn to patronize, a staff told me "no paywave", only cash, nets, paynow and fairprice app. So in the end, made the payment thru Fairprice App.

When come about to patronise another food stall, since the place is under "Kopitiam" company, since all stalls have their barcode scanners, so I try to use the fairprice app for payment but was told cant use the app to pay. Only cash or QR code payment.

Is very confusing that one can use card payment, awhile later cant and one can use app payment, another one cant.

This place alone, eaten off the stars rating.

Quite a few stalls not operating today.
Looking forward trying the duck rice stall another day since is closed.

Spacious and well ventilated hawker centre with a wide variety of food. Sheltered car park is located conveniently below the hawker centre. There is also a community book exchange corner. Clean and big toilets are a bonus.
Ying Jia Steamed Fish - fresh (not frozen) fish & blanched leafy veg. They prepare on the spot. Bonus is it's v affordable! Unusual in HDB hawker centre. Wifey loves it!

I love the variety of food you can chooses and the place is very huge and clean. Newly open food court since Nov 2023, located at Level 1 of Tower 2 within Surbana Jurong Campus, currently there are 3 stalls which are selling mixed vegetable rice, chicken rice and a beverage s(also offer beers, fruits, snacks and pau). Food offered from all 3 stalls is pretty good and the atmosphere is great. Welcoming addition of food choices for the area and do note that it might get pretty crowded during lunch time. In addition, the campus also offers free parking on Mon-Fri 6pm-11.59pm and Sat 7am-2pm.

Good ranges for food from economic rice, noodles, chicken rice, curry noodles and mala xiang guo and nice coffee/tea… a little pricy for some items…

This cozy corner lot in SJ Campus is situated on the 1st floor of Tower 2. The friendly service and welcoming owner make the experience enjoyable, but the highlight is the fresh and healthy food. While the prices may be on the higher side, they are reasonable for a nutritious meal. Customers can choose from five basic toppings for $6 or six for $7, with optional add-ons like salmon, chicken karaage, and more. Pro tip: Arrive early to place your order and pick it up later to avoid the lunchtime rush starting at noon.

Finally got to try the new Coconut Taro Smoothie!! Love the coconut flavour and also loving the fresh taro and red bean toppings that's included inside 🔥🔥 Able to choose sugar level too 👍 Definitely will come back to get it again!
This is really good! Fusilli pasta with fresh salad and the sauce is simply breathtaking. Mango smoothie is refreshing smooth!
Super yummy and worth it!

Can’t wait to taste their salad bowl, tea and acai bowl. Also wish there’s a few vegan pastries and desserts on their menu!

There’s waiting area to sit at while your salad is being prepared. Or you can preorder it too!

I got my hands on their basic salad bowl.
They’re generous with the sauces. I love how their salad ordering sheet is categorised, so you can choose the veggies and flavours going into your bowl.

They ran out of roasted sweet potato pretty quickly. So I swapped it with potato instead.
I feel it’s a lil too salty for me. But if your salt tolerance is high, you won’t feel it.

There’s staff discount for people working in Surbana Jurong building .

No dining area.

The ice cream and waffles are tasty! I had cookies monster and waffles. The service was also commendable. Good neighbourhood location too. Nice place for friends to hangout.Thanks to Yuai and Yuan Sheng for accommodating to my request. Cheers!

really enjoyed their gelato and waffles! the waffles were crisp at the edges but had a chewy centre, which made every bite really enjoyable 😋 the hojicha gelato had a strong tea fragrance without being too sweet, while the pistachio one was another good and safe choice.

for a sweet & savoury combination, you can even get their mentaiko tater tots that were freshly fried and went perfectly with the umami mentaiko sauce 🍟🥔

Crispy, golden waffle topped with a scoop of creamy ice cream—simple, delicious, and perfectly balanced. Warm meets cold in the best way. Definitely a must-try.

Great waffle café with a cozy vibe. Friendly service, and fair prices. Definitely worth a visit!
Great friendly and fast service. The waffle is nice but abit dry today. Anyways hoping for paddlepop flavour!!
really amazing one of a kind waffles and unique ice cream flavours and super conducive to chill or study  will come back again! 🍨

I chanced upon this bistro while browsing Google Maps this morning and decided to pay it a visit after work.

The mushroom soup was delicious, with a rich and creamy consistency. The mushroom flavour was well balanced, giving it a savoury and comforting taste.

The topshell had a springy and succulent texture with a delicate sweetness from the seafood. Every bite was fresh and satisfying.

The BBQ baby pork ribs were fall-off-the-bone tender, glazed with a smoky and slightly sweet barbecue sauce that delivered a rich and satisfying flavour in every bite.

Overall, it was a pleasant dining experience and a nice find for an after-work meal.

Googled this place for an escape lunch — trying new places and exploring new makan experiences. It had a solid 4.5 ⭐️ review, so expectations were naturally there.

My personal experience:

1. Off-the-beaten-track charm with a rustic bar vibe. There’s a large pond in view from the restaurant, which gave the place a very peaceful and charming atmosphere overall.

2. Indoor air-conditioned seating made for a nice escape from our scorching weather. The décor was modest. There was a high-table area beside large windows that unfortunately ended up being used to store loose items. Not exactly an eyesore, but it did take away a little from the overall aesthetic charm. The bar area itself looked pretty cute though.

3. F&B felt overpriced considering the location, especially since it’s not the most accessible place by public transport.

4. We ordered:
- Yuzu honey — tasted good, similar to those sold in Korean stores.
- Iced chocolate — pleasant enough, though I’m almost certain it came from a sachet mix.
- Mushroom soup — tasty, but way too watered down.
- Beer battered cod — acceptable overall. The batter was harder than expected, though the fries were actually quite nice: slim and crispy.

Overall, I don’t think I’d make a special trip down just to dine here again.

Total damage for 2 cod, 1 soup and 2 drinks came up to about $90 after GST and service charge.

This bar is a little out of the way, especially for those taking public transport. Despite the location, prices of alcohol were not competitive. A pint of mainstream beer costs $12.50-15++. But do check out their Happy Hours on Mon-Thu, 12-8pm. Ambience was OK. The owner Mark was very friendly and chatty.

Came on a Sunday afternoon, there was only one other table around. Weather was blazing hot so settled for indoor seats. Will imagine that if the weather is cooler or nearing sunset, the outdoor seats should be some vibes of such, with the dining area overlooking a mini fish pond. Ordered their ribs, carbonara, wings, clams pasta, fish and chips and truffle fries. Wings impressed us the most! Was well marinated and piping hot and crispy when served. The pitcher iced tea is also a good deal and so good on a sweltering hot day!

I ordered chicken chop. The food was well plated, portions were generally generous and most importantly delicious. The frenches were fried golden and very crispy too! Yummy.

Went there on a hot afternoon, and weated throughout my lunch, LOL. Felt stuffy and hot despite only 2 tables of customers.

The waitress forgot our drinks orders and was always trying to rush off before we complete our sentences / orders. But overall was we got our food.

Tasty and aromatic, every bite brightens up my day!
dons are super worth it and yummy - mackrel and beef esp! go to place to treat myself after exams / tests, great hangout area w best matcha and hojicha (: gem id 100% recommend!

Located a tad bit out of the way from the well-trafficked areas of NTU's North Spine and I've only known this place rather recently. Surprisingly popular among some students and staffs - there is always crowds around lunch time, perhaps the 10% discount of all items on sale for them is one of the main draws.

Discount aside, the offerings are actually pretty good given the price, albeit the Japanese and Japanese-inspired noodle dishes' portion is somewhat smaller than average for most people. The coffee is one of the very bests in NTU and very competitively priced ($3 for long black/espresso after discount). Don't expect the variety like real speciality coffee place like let's say Venture Drive, but they are know what they are doing - the coffee I've had was chocolaty, a bit velvety, and came with right 'body' and thickness.

Due to its popularity, the indoor space is very crowded in lunch time, but the outdoor dining space (next to a rooftop garden) is almost always empty. Some shading (even patio table with umbrella) would do, especially in hot weather.

Really good place to study! Their coffee is good. $3.50 for a black, which isn’t bad. They have a 10% discount for NTU students.
Both indoor and outdoor is really nice. It gets busy around 11am. Some outlets, but not many.
Nice coffee and food! Cute latte art, might get crowded during lunch hour and need to avoid studying here

The restaurant is neat, clean, and has a nice ambiance. The food was good, but since I wasn't very hungry, I didn't try the full meal. However, fellow diners commented that it was good.

Yew Tee Point is located outside the Yew Tee MRT Station. It's very easy to miss because of the ongoing renovation that is happening in the middle of the square.

Unlike the square, this place is air-conditioned. It's like a big open space with many things happening in the middle including stalls, merchandize displays, and F&B. It gives the vibe of older style malls but somehow feels comfy.

affordable prices and fresh groceries workers were also friendly as well as the handsome boss

amazing service, boss so friendly 👏🏻 many goods of gd quality 😆

The cashier Saranya is very friendly. She greets with a smile and initiates small chats. The place looks very clean and organised. Kudos to her!
Say open 8.30, 8.41 still closed. Where is bro💀
