Starmart is a standalone mart sharing the place with Caltex. Such marts are often compared to 7 eleven or Cheers. However, the prices of things at Starmart seem to be cheaper. The Pokka drinks are cheaper here for sure. And there's more variety of goods here. I can get Meiji and Rittersport chocolates here. By the way, there's even restrooms inside the mart.

place_name: Quan Shui Wet Market @ Upper Thomson
review_text:
Tried some of their food during boutique fair 2025. It was ok, but it’s really nothing special considering the price. The har gao and siew Mai are quite mushy. Kong Bak Pao isn’t fantastic either, just normal. Noodles and dumpling are alright.

Tried their food again at their original location and still not impressed with it especially the Har Gao. Skin is too thick and meat is not substantial. The rest are also mediocre.

place_name: Quan Shui Wet Market @ Upper Thomson
review_text:
Bought the festive premium pork feasting platter for christmas and the only thing that is premium is the word premium printed on the label. I followed the instructions on the packaging for preparation. Instructions were easy to follow, but that was the only good thing in this meal.

The ribs were extremely tough - worse than the ones you get at cold storage. Under seasoned and had to add my own bbq sauce to make it somewhat edible. Ate 2 ribs and threw the rest away.

The porchetta is fatty and bland. Ate one mouthful and threw the rest away.

Sausages were too salty.

Meatballs were ok, but you can probably get the same quality from the super market.

For $82, you’re better off spending your money elsewhere. I dare say Mcdonald’s and Saizeriya tastes better than this.

This might be the worst Christmas meal I’ve ever had in my life. Perhaps maybe even one of the worst meals I’ve ever had in my life.

The company should stop selling these sets immediately.

Would have given -100 stars on google reviews if google allowed me to, but I guess I’ll have to settle for 1 star which in my opinion, is too generous.

Thanks for ruining my Christmas. You guys should rename your company to “The Grinch”.

place_name: Quan Shui Wet Market @ Upper Thomson
review_text: Ah Ma’s handmade Yong Tau Foo it’s really bad. I couldn’t even go for second bite. The fish paste inside was really hard as well.

place_name: Quan Shui Wet Market @ Upper Thomson
review_text:
First time online ordering from them. Usually I went to the physical store.

Have to compliment them on how well the items were packed. The green were in a recycle bag And there’s ice pack in between each layers of frozen items.

My daughter loves their Siew Mai and I love the garlic chilli. 🥰 Oh! The pork ribs are really fresh too. Used it to cook bbq ribs and it’s really yummy.
Trying some new items this round. Looking forward to more purchase with them.

place_name: Quan Shui Wet Market @ Upper Thomson
review_text: The Signature Pineapple Tart tin is not sealed. Inside it is a ziplock bag (also with no safety seal) containing the tarts. Quality of the tarts is below expectation too, since the small tin is priced at almost $28. Feels like this shop had bought some cheap tarts and repacked it for resale.
Very good experience at Empwer fitness on a Monday noon strength session. Highly recommend for all the women’s who want to feel strong during 1 hour.

Since joining the hyrox group training at Empower Fitness I’ve seen major improvements in performance. Lizzie’s advice has greatly improved my station work, and the structured classes have really helped my compromised running. The coaching is excellent and so are the results. Highly recommend!

If you're training for Hyrox, this is a great place to workout. They have the same equipment, stations measured at the correct specs, new equipment, etc.

I just wish they had more openings in ClassPass, I always have to set a very early alarm just to catch a spot. Other than that, loved the workout!

I had such a fantastic experience training with Lizzie! She's extremely professional, kind, and full of great energy. She always goes above and beyond to help and make sure you're taken care of. I booked the Hyrox 90-minute training without much idea of what to expect, and despite how tough the workout was, I thoroughly enjoyed it thanks to the friendly and encouraging environment Lizzie has created. Couldn't have asked for a better trainer! Thank you, Lizzie!

I couldn't be more happy with my experience at Empower. It's an absolute neighborhood gem that exudes a friendly and welcoming atmosphere. The trainers and members create a tight-knit community that's incredibly supportive, making every group workout enjoyable and motivating. The gym‘s group classes specifically on Hyrox training is great, as it effectively prepares you to tackle the challenge. Highly recommended for anyone seeking an exceptional gym to reach their fitness goals!

This is the main building of University Sports Centre, also known as USC. Located opposite Yusof Ishak House, this three-storey facility boasts features like swimming pools, squash courts, a gymnasium, and a rock-climbing wall. It’s also effectively a sheltered path for those travelling to Valour House from the direction of YIH.
Home of Team NUS. There are plenty of sports facilities here including swimming pool and squash courts.

The sports centre houses a variety of sports facilities, including two olympic-sized swimming pools (indoor and outdoor), gym, squash, among others.

The swimming pool is generally clean, and may get busy at times. I also particularly appreciate the clean changing rooms/toilet.
Well managed sports centre with vibrant community.
Sports complex for basketball, swimming, squash, table tennis etc. for NUS students and staff.

Such a little jewel of a cinema in Singapore. It has a wonderfully quaint, retro charm that makes the whole experience feel special and nostalgic in the best way. In a time when so many cinemas feel generic, this place has real character and soul. The atmosphere is intimate, cosy, and full of old-school charm, which makes watching a film here feel far more memorable. Definitely the kind of hidden gem that deserves more love and support. A must-visit for anyone who appreciates unique independent cinemas. We really gotta support places like this!
Lovely cinema! Really love the retro aesthetic of the lobby. Glad that Filmhouse is carrying on The Projector's legacy of providing a space for alternative cinema!

I am glad that there's a place like Filmhouse in Singapore to appreciate and support quality movies for the movie-fans. The selection of movies that Filmhouse is choosing to screen seem to be artistic and award-winning ones - both from the past and newly released. This is definitely a place for movie fans to come from time to time. The seats are pretty small and uncomfortable, but good for the old-school vibes. Their toilets are really cool with many movie posters too.

Just one thing; if you are tall (like above 175cm), please do not take first half rows. You will be blocking the subtitles. Be considerate and sit at the back.
Great place, chill vibes, and friendly people. A bunch of cultured cinema goers’ heaven. Classics being screened every so often, definitely recommend.
Good movie theatre with nostalgic taste! And their popcorn is very nice!

It is a vibrant hub offering food like Burger King, a gym, basketball and table tennis courts, and more. It hosts events such as career fairs and volunteer opportunities. Conveniently located near West Coast Park, Clementi Woods Park, Kent Ridge Secondary School, West Coast Market, and West Coast Plaza, it serves as an active and accessible spot for residents to eat, exercise, and participate in community activities.
There is a lion dance troupe that practices every Friday at 7

Exchanged my steps tracker there by appointment. Had my trace together token exchanged due to flat battery too. A convenient and airy space. There is a fast food outlet there. There are ATM machines around too. A good location to book private hired cars too.

Review: September 2022
Staff at the CC are polite & helpful & hope that it remains such.  The  management should regulate training offen & remind their role in community center .   The facilities are getting better and useful for all.
The POSB VTM alongside the ATM'S.
Video teller machine (VTM) is an electronic banking outlet that allows customers to complete basic transactions, request banking services or receive immediate assistance with the help of live video conferencing consultation with a remote bank teller.

Review : November 2021
The staff working aren't trained well in their interacting skills , more offen than not during my visits which I've noticed.
Just as I was registering my entry,  distance away from those seated staff & they questioned me as to what I wanted .
The committee club is obviously for everyone who needs to be updated or for a particular cause.
The staff should be well mannered & ethical to handle any visitor , by NOT being prejudice.

Great place for community activities, event hostings. There is a big hall, basket ball court. Do check out for the upcoming events on the notice board. Extra curricular activities, dance, music classes are also held for children. Had a chance to view the FIFA World Cup 2022 final screening.

🤼Jurong Eco Garden (The Potter's Garden) Children Playground consist of two sections catering to children of different ages.
🛝Located at the Central Plaza, the sand-filled Playground meant for 5-12 yrs had an impressive Rope Pyramid Climber complete with a Tube Slide that is linked by a rope bridge.
🧗A set of 4 Swings sits beside the Rope Pyramid Climber.
🛝At another shaded section, just in front of the Central Plaza, where the toilets are housed, is a Wooden Climbing Structure with an Open Slide for younger kids 2-5 yrs.
🤹There is also a See-Saw and a Merry-go-Round to keep the kids happy.
🪑Plenty of seats around the Playground area, so parents and caregivers can sit around to watch over their children.
🚿Water fountains and washing point are conveniently located at the Playground area.
👯A great place for children to have fun.

Fresh air, peace ambience with occasional sounds of soothing birds singing rhythmically. More needs to be done to entice nature lovers to appreciate this park. It was a Sunday morning when I first explored this park and I was a bit surprised to see not many visitors stepped into this park and immerse in such lovely place to unwind before we kick start another new day in the coming week.

Great for walking and jogging.
There's an eco garden near by. Watch out for wild animals like monkeys and boars.
Green snakes are quite common too.
There's a toilet and water dispenser available as well.
Scenic and photography spot for photographers to capture the sun and rare birds that visit this part of Singapore.
nice and quite place. But no sufficient food court nearby.
best wishes
Wonderful place @ clean tech...

Kallang Park Connector is an absolute gem for anyone who appreciates a bit of wilderness in the heart of the city. As a regular spot for jogging, it offers much more than just a paved path; the air is incredibly fresh, making those morning or evening runs feel revitalizing. What really sets this route apart, though, is the incredible biodiversity. During my visits, I’ve encountered everything from vibrant insects to the occasional snakes in the river—nature is definitely thriving here.

For those who find themselves constantly looking toward the water, the sightings of various fish species are a fantastic bonus that adds to the charm of the area. It’s the perfect blend of a functional fitness route and a mini-safari. Whether you're working on your cardio or just looking to clear your head, the connection to nature here is top-notch. If you're looking for an all-in-one spot to stay active and enjoy the outdoors, this is the place to be. Highly recommended for nature enthusiasts and fitness lovers alike!

Kallang Park Connector is a linear green trail in Singapore’s Park Connector Network (PCN) that runs along the Kallang River and links Bishan-Ang Mo Kio Park in the north to Kallang Riverside Park in the south.

It’s about 7.87 km long and provides a scenic route for walking, jogging, and cycling through several central residential areas

We definitely enjoyed our walk along the Kallang PCN.

There's a weird sign pointing towards bishan park (the wrong way), confused us for a moment.

One of the most scenic park connector in Singapore linking Clark Quay and City center to Kallang, Kallang Bahru, Potong Pasir and all the way to Bishan Park. Perfect for cycling, jogging and brisk walking.
Very good area for relax your mind

The 2 stretches of Kallang Park connector that go under the bridges are finally open this week! Lovely artwork. Kudos to the artists that put them up and pat on the back for the workers whp were involved in the construction.  Joggers and cyclists can now bypass 2 traffic lights at Kallang Bahru and Upper Boon Keng Road, to get from Bendemeer Road to Kallang MRT. Celebration!

Our son and daughter both attended Rain Trees and they loved every moment of their time there. It provides a wonderfully nurturing and engaging environment for children and ours were genuinely excited to go to school every morning.  The teachers are exceptional, warm and really care for the children.  The grounds are beautiful and provide the children with an amazing space for outdoor play as well as the beautiful indoor classrooms.  We were particularly impressed with the Mandarin programme.  The class sizes are ideal and both our children and us made fantastic friends through the Rain Trees community.

Raintrees has been the perfect preparation for our daughter for international school. Rohini and her team are experienced educators and were invested in her development journey right from the very first meeting and throughout her time there.  The team were available, flexible and gave us valuable advice and continual communication on her progress throughout her time there.  Most importantly, it is a nurturing, fun and beautiful learning environment that our daughter was delighted to go to every day and has come away with great friends. lovely memories and ready for her next step.

We started our son at Rain Trees March 2025. It has been the best education experience for our son and a great ride for us as parents. We selected Rain Trees for its commitment to educate a diverse group of children particularly focusing on building independence, confidence, sense of query, curiosity, creativity and of course early childhood numeracy and literacy skills. Rain Trees is one of the best schools in Singapore that provides all the above at a very high standard. Educators at Rain Trees are attentive, knowledgeable and supportive. They have helped our son to grow at his pivotal moments in his life. School leaders are amazing as well for their vision, ability to execute, and their strong beliefs in education. We saw our son thrive at Rain Trees and would highly recommend the school to anyone who’s looking for a dynamic, supportive, diverse, and vibrant learning environment for the children.
The school is full of details that show the educators’ love. They have really meaningful and engaging activities and events for both children and the parents all year long. I also particularly love their outdoor environment - the beautiful back garden they have just provided lots of fun and opportunities for children to enjoy and explore!
Overall a huge thumbs up! Highly recommend!

My daughter absolutely loves her time at Rain Trees. She adores her teachers, Ms. Jong and Ms. Anna, who have been so kind, caring, and supportive. Principal Rohani is also incredibly attentive and thoughtful, which means a lot to us as parents.

One of the clearest signs of how happy she is there is that she even wants to go back to school on weekends. The school is set in a beautiful standalone colonial-style house with a spacious outdoor area where the children get to play every day.

What we especially appreciate is how the school celebrates festivals from different cultures, creating such a warm, inclusive, and international environment for the children. It is a wonderful place for kids to learn about diversity and make friends from many different countries.

We were also deeply touched when my daughter was hospitalized and her teacher came to visit her with a big card filled with messages from the whole class. It was such a thoughtful and heartfelt gesture, and it showed us how much the school truly cares for every child.

I would highly recommend Rain Trees to any parent, especially expat families who have just moved to Singapore. It is a loving, welcoming community where children feel happy, supported, and truly at home.

One of the standout international preschools in Singapore. Our daughter joined at 18 months, and we saw remarkable progress within a short span — from early language development to confidently reciting the alphabet and numbers before she turned two. More importantly, she genuinely loves going to school, which speaks volumes about the environment Raintrees creates.

The school offers a truly diverse and nurturing setting, and it has been wonderful to see her build friendships across cultures at such a young age. The teaching staff are exceptionally attentive in understanding each child’s personality, emotional needs, and pace of development.

Rohini Ramadas, the Principal, brings deep expertise in early childhood education, and every interaction with her has been thoughtful, reassuring, and highly professional. Her leadership is clearly reflected in the quality and consistency of the school’s approach.

A special mention to her class teachers, Anna Facey and Jong Jong Say, who are outstanding — warm, hands-on, and genuinely invested in each child’s growth.

Raintrees also has a strong reputation among leading international schools in Singapore, and the team provided invaluable support throughout our daughter’s application process, helping her secure a place at one of the top schools.

Overall, we couldn’t have asked for a better start to her learning journey.
only in ohio boiii ts so tufff

Made of friends and a bit okay memories bet just go to sparkle tots honestly this school is just a normal school they do fun events but there's  rarely  school trips and the worst part is that there's no programmes and the teachers are mid like when I studied here the teacher scolded me often and we only went to school trip once even though it was before covid

all my kids went to this childcare when they were young. they have great teachers who impacted my kids till this day. (they are always talking about teacher sabrina and 李老师) and also terrific lessons like teaching the kids basic math and all which really helped my kids when they entered primary school. (my kids had great achievements in primary school and even went to gep). really a very good preschool to send your child to
One of the best Child care! I like the rigor they put into their curriculum.
Attended ACJC's traditional biennial funfair. Such an exciting event! Kudos to all the students and teachers who made the event so grand.

Not only does this school love sucking out money from its students to allegedly maintain facilities that 1. students NEVER get to use and 2. NEVER become cleaner or better in condition, they also enjoy developing bladder diseases in their poor students.

Teachers, especially the discipline master, ridicule and punish, sometimes even forbid, students from simply using the toilet. A 5 minute trip to the toilet to empty one’s bladder from holding it in for hours is seen as an act of sin, a crime being committed by this school that praises itself for being so loving with the grace of God.

During chapel, assembly, or school events where students are gathered in the hall, using to the toilet is seen as a threat, a crime. Students will either be held captive in another room where they will not even be able to see what is going on in the main hall to appreciate the event, will be told to take the route that is much longer to come back to the hall (which makes absolutely no sense as one would have to walk past much more people if their reason is disturbing the course of event), or simply, told no and scolded for “not being able to hold it in”.

Teachers say to students in a humiliating, condescending tone that if they cannot hold their bladder for more than a few hours, they should “get it checked”. This is either implying that students should not stay hydrated or using the toilet is undesirable, when it is part and parcel of how the normal human body works. Over time, with students not being able to use the restroom when they urgently need it, kidney and bladder diseases may be manifested. Urinary Tract Infections are amongst one of the many diseases students would have a much higher chance of having after holding in their urine for extended periods of time just to adhere to some school expectations. Not only that, it is simply extremely uncomfortable sitting still with a full bladder.

I sincerely hope the school will be more considerate of students’ health and needs and be more understanding.

I went to ACJC this morning at 11:30am as I wanted to purchase a swimming paddle for my child. At the entrance, the security guard Mr. Liao asked me to register and requested for my IC. I simply asked him whether it was necessary to provide my IC, but instead of explaining politely, he responded in a rude manner and told me not to “talk too much,” threatening not to let me in and even challenging me to complain to the authorities.

I believe such an attitude is not appropriate, especially within a school environment that should set an example for students in communication and respect. If students were to overhear such an exchange, what impression would they be left with?

I sincerely hope the school can look into this matter and ensure better communication and professionalism from the staff. Your improvement will be very much appreciated.

Wonderful Junior College providing a holistic education. Tremendously benefitted from my experience here at ACJC in 2003-2004, with lifelong friendships and bonds still going strong 20+ years later. Blessed to be part of the ACSian family, with teachers and even our former Principal being ever supportive of our individual endeavours. The sports facilities were fantastic too from the track to the swimming pool and gym, I credit a lot of my sporting journey from this too. Thank you, ACJC! 🙏🏻

The location is strategic, as it sits between 2 mrt stations, and surrounded by many eateries and shopping malls. The sport complex is big, there's football field, running track, swimming pools, as well as badminton court.
Amazing campus, aircon at most areas. Well maintained landscapes.

This is the latest polytechnic in Singapore. It is spacious. The buildings are quite new. Well maintenance.

It has a hospitality school too.

The location is next to Woodlands North MRT TEL. It is so convenient. From Woodlands interchange, just board 169.

I am currently 6 months into a part-time software engineering diploma, and it has been an eye opening adventure. Although I haven't reached the finish line yet, the journey has been filled with growth and discovery. This program is a fantastic fit for anyone curious about the digital world; while a little coding background helps, the most important requirement is a willingness to learn.

Because many of us balance this course with full-time jobs, the lecturers are incredibly supportive. They truly "get it." I’ve found the environment to be one where curiosity is celebrated; no question is too small. The instructors are remarkably open, always searching for the most creative and clear ways to help us grasp complex ideas.

Even as I continue working toward my completion, I can already see how much this experience has reshaped my thinking. It’s more than just learning to code it’s about finding new ways to solve problems.

Clean and modern environment.
Great atmosphere for students.
Good and clear motor vehicles routes map.

Is Republic Polytechnic Closed Down?

Lack of response to my enquiry. I sent an email days ago.

In addition, I tried calling the main contact number and was left on hold for over 30 minutes without any assistance. This level of service is unacceptable. Our time as customers is equally valuable and should be respected.

I strongly urge your management to look into this matter and ensure that proper communication and responsiveness are maintained moving forward.

place_name: NUS Hon Sui Sen Memorial Library
review_text: Excellent library. Enough space, comfortable chairs, bookable study rooms and no-click areas for more intensive readings. Access only with matriculation card.

place_name: NUS Hon Sui Sen Memorial Library
review_text: saya suke sangat kat hon sui sen memorial library

place_name: NUS Hon Sui Sen Memorial Library
review_text: good place to study

place_name: NUS Hon Sui Sen Memorial Library
review_text: Heaven for knowledge

place_name: NUS Hon Sui Sen Memorial Library
Comics Library at West Coast Community Centre
review_text:
To use this library you need to register your passion card or ez link first. Talk to the community center guy for more help.
It looks like they brought all of the books from Anchor point

place_name: Comics Library at West Coast Community Centre
review_text:
Library is in the community centre. Large collection of comics. Kids can read there or borrow books home.
Regsitration to enter is more cumbersome though... need to register 3 days prior to visit, or onsite before 6pm. Hope they can look into that to allow more spontaneous registration.
Borrowing is through NLB app, so all is good!

place_name: Comics Library at West Coast Community Centre

review_text: Bus stop had moved nearer to pedestrian crossing junction
It's a normal bus stop.
Nice place.
There's a bus stop across the street from Fort Canning Park, and rental bikes are parked nearby.
Sometimes bus driver just drive past because we cannot see the bus.
greeneries before the NUH

It is a LRT station in Sengkang. It is part of the West bound area.

It definitely helps people to commute faster.

It ferries passengers to Sengkang station which is interconnected with Sengkang MRT and Compass Point shopping mall as well as bus interchange.

Not much of facilities unlike MRT station. There are ezlink top up machines. There is a lift for everyone including the elderly, disabled, child's pram, and anyone who carry heavier weight of items like luggages.

A well planned build up that you can avoid rain with shelter if you stay at Anchorvale. But recent hike in amounts of resident due to opening of new HDB. The queue are very long and rate of the lrt arrival time had not change could cause you a long wait. Imagine during a hot season with high humidity.

Opened on 15 November 2007 as part of the Sengkang LRT line west loop operated by SBS Transit, this LRT station is located at Anchorvale Street near the junction with Anchorvale Crescent and Anchorvale Road and is located near Sengkang ActiveSG Sports Centre, Sengkang Hockey Stadium, Springdale Primary School, Anchorvale Community Club, Sengkang Riverside Park and Anchorvale Village (which includes a hawker centre which is managed by Kopitiam) and brings direct rail connectivity for residents of Sengkang Neighbourhood 3 (Anchorvale Cove, Anchorvale Parkview, Anchorvale Horizon, Anchorvale Harvest) and Bellewaters bringing them along to Sengkang Town Centre, Sengkang ActiveSG Sports Centre, Anchorvale Village and Sengkang Riverside Park. The public bus services serving Farmway LRT station are SMRT Buses service 110 between Compassvale Bus Interchange and Changi Airport and SBS Transit City Direct service 654 between Anchorvale Street and Shenton Way and the station is named after farms which were formerly in the Sengkang area back in the past. The old roads in the Sengkang area were referred to as Farmways, such as Cheng Lim Farmways 1 to 6 in the area.
Better connectivity to senkang west

wait can be up to 6 minutes during off peak hours
while usually 4 during off peak and 2 during peak, sometimes the train that comes in between during off peak is off service so it may go up to 8-10 minutes
though it happens at abt 3-4 so not much ppl are affected

place_name: Hong Lim Curry Puff @ Jurong West Hawker Center

review_text: This food court is located at the top floor of Kent Ridge MRT Station. Environment is clean and spacious. Teh was good. However, food were expensive for the average taste and quality.

Not the best food quality in my experience hit and miss in some store.  The BCM store is it a hybrid I never seen a fried meatball in the noodles and the taste of the seasoning not good enough  Cai fan probably the best most friendly keep asking everybody to come because they got a lot of variety the rest of the store not much to offer. Limited hala meal. Some store can’t tell if it’s hala base on offering

The bak chor mee from the stall Noodle Master was not bad but it’s too spicy. The stall operator didn’t bother to ask if we needed it to be spicy, tomato, or plain. Looks like they lack experience. It’s also not a traditional bak chor mee as they added two fried meat balls. The meatballs were nice but one of them was cold inside. The noodles was cooked to perfect texture and that’s a plus point!

Often eat at this food court when go to NUH. Food surprisingly is very good. Hv tried the basil pork rice @Viet Meet Thai stall, curry charsiew noodle @Curry Chicken stall; minced pork noodle @Ah Hwa Bak Chor Mee; mix vegetable; etc.. Very recomended especially the curry noodle & porkball noodle

Clean environment but it’s quite warm. Food is not bad. I usually order Fishball noodles, western, Thai food. Priced fairly amongst other food places nearby. Lunch time during weekdays can be super packed and hard to find seats.

Kopitiam at NUH Main building.
I tried the food from a few stalls and i think the Nasi Padang stall's mee robus was good; Wok delight we ordered Pineapple Rice and Mee Goreng, both average. My mee goreng tasted like hokkien mee though 😅 ; Popiah used to be pretty good but previous order was not up to standard, was too wet and a little bland. Penyet was good but you must order when the chicken freshly out from frying pan. I went one late evening around 8pm, the chicken was lukewarm and it was not nice at all. I was very disappointed.
As for beverages, i am ok with their coffee and tea!

Ordered Buddha Jump Over The Wall, not to my expectation. The soup don't taste like what I usually had. Quite tasteless this time round.
Adding of hot water cost me $0.30. Daylight robbery... Think should have gone out to outside normal coffeeshop could have cheaper.

Recommend the western stall, you get what you paid for, large portions/servings.

Had the salmon pasta set meal, with mushroom soup & fries, salmon was large but thin fillet, otherwise taste was good. 😋

Would recommend the previous meal I had last visit, the fried chicken cutlet set meal & upgrade to chees fries, that 1 the fried fillet was crispy & thick.👍👍
Peanut lotus root soup from lao Huo tang is good. Would be even better if they can give more meat.

Ordered the Mee Kia with chicken cutlet from the noodles shop, Portion was decent but the noodles were a little plain and not well cooked. The chicken cutlet was fried to order, so it was okay, served warm but not the juicy or tender kind. Nothing too special. Theres 10% off if you use FairPrice app! So this was $4.50! Can try!

Exceptionally a perfect getaway peaceful place!
The charming small cafe is located in the same building with primary school in Clementi Woods Park, a pleasant greenery peaceful place for hiking, exploring natures in the breeze morning and a delightful brunch.

The food exceeded my expectations. The savory waffle was absolutely delicious! The Egg Benedict had poached eggs cooked with bagel sesame, perfectly done.

The waffle was thick and chewy, satisfying in texture, and went perfectly with the toppings. The owner was creativity in the dishes and excellent coffee.💞☕️

Highly recommended to soak in a personalized and exceptional dining experience!💞

The café is run by one person, who puts great care into both the coffee and the food, and is very warm and welcoming to customers. Today, on her recommendation, I tried her homemade cookies with coffee—absolutely delicious. And, my breakfast was great too!
It’s located just down from the car park, next to a kindergarten, inside Woods Park. It’s a quiet and peaceful spot, perfect for relaxing and enjoying a nice cup of coffee.
I found a great spot today.

Quaint and cozy cafe tucked away in Clementi Woods! The owner is a 1-man show but she's very friendly and the food is delicious (see how I emptied the plate 😆) I ordered the grilled cheese and ham sandwich.
If you stay nearby it's worth making a trip down :) But note they are closed on weekends and otherwise open from 8am-4.30pm!

Auntie Doris is very sweet and caring. I went there with my parents and we had lengthy conversation and very good food. Not a second we felt like we are in a Cafe, but it was more like a home we were invited to have a breakfast in. Very silent atmosphere with soft music inside this play school pulled me back into my childhood.

A very cute and cozy cafe tucked away in the corner of a building next to a preschool! I went to grab lunch and study, and the lady was very kind and friendly. My friend had a similar experience where he went and ended up talking with the lady there for a long time. The grilled cheese and ham sandwich was delicious and I really enjoyed the atmosphere there. I wish I had more time to visit again!
It's a rushed atmosphere, serving the foreign workers community at a Dormitory.
Food is the awesome. Service very good
People who love Bangladesh food, can try mutton biriani there.  Alot of desert also available.
Good place to chill at night. Quiet and relaxing. You have the whole coffee shop to yourself.
The slim Indian young guy rather rude, even don’t allow his customer to take the plastic spoon

place_name: Wee's Family Coffee Shop (黄家咖啡店)
review_text: I visited the place after reading the post from IeatIShootIpost, and I wasn't disappointed. The food was really good, with large portions at reasonable prices. Our favorite dish was the homemade tofu! The curry fish head was tasty but a bit on the sweet side. The fried beef was incredibly tender. Unfortunately, we couldn't try the pig trotter and sassam fish because our group was small. I definitely plan to return to sample the rest of their recommended dishes. The coffee shop setting is a little bit small though .

place_name: Wee's Family Coffee Shop (黄家咖啡店)
review_text: Step into this Makansutra-rated eatery for a zhi char feast that won't break the bank. With a varied menu, every dish is a treat, packed with flavor from quality ingredients and served in generous portions.

place_name: Wee's Family Coffee Shop (黄家咖啡店)
review_text:
25/September/2025
Had sambal kang kong, bitter gourd with fish head, salted fish with sliced pork & 1 bowl of steamed rice ($50.70) for dinner, was good.
I especially enjoyed the salted fish with sliced pork (咸鱼不见天). Service was friendly by the senior uncle with recommendations. Al fresco dining with adequate tables.

place_name: Wee's Family Coffee Shop (黄家咖啡店)
review_text: Price is reasonable. All the dishes are good for me and my family 👍!

place_name: Wee's Family Coffee Shop (黄家咖啡店)
review_text: The food is sooooo good for a family coffee shop! Must try dishes - cereal prawn, salted egg bittergourd! Didn't manage to take photo because they were too good and we ate them too fast. Convenient to order, can just call and order then do pick up.

The Star Vista is one of the more spacious and pleasant malls in the Buona Vista area. I like the open layout, high ceilings, wide walkways, and the large central atrium, which makes the mall feel airy and less cramped compared with many other shopping centres

There is a good variety of restaurants and cafes here, ranging from casual meals to dessert places and family-friendly dining options. The recently opened Yo-Chi also seems very popular, with a long queue and lively crowd, adding more buzz to the mall

Another plus point is The Star Performing Arts Centre, which makes the place more than just a regular mall. It is a convenient spot for meals before or after events, and the overall environment feels clean, spacious and comfortable

Right next to the Buona Vista MRT station.
Convenient, airy ,spacious with 3 solid floors of eateries, cafes ,restaurants & supermarket.
If you are a owner of a weak mind you will die of over eating here !
In Chinese they say 多元化选择性障碍症 !

Christmas season is here again!
Children would think of Santa Claus, Christmas tree, snowman, Christmas gifts...
Christmas is about the birth of Jesus Christ, born in a wooden stable. But we often see a different scene. A very cooling beautiful white Christmas.
Still, this seaon is a good time to bond, to love, to share...
My family,relatives and I feasted in 'The Star' at Bouna Vista. Good food, good atmosphere, good environment
see the snow falling together. Wow! Great feeling.

Star Vista: An architectural landmark with a unique layout.

I visited the Star Vista at 2 PM on a weekend, and the mall was lively and crowded with people. From a distance, the building is very impressive with its bold, highlighted design.

The Layout & Shops:
Once inside, the layout of the stalls feels a bit more "legacy" compared to the modern exterior. There is a large staircase on the first floor which is a great spot to sit and rest for a while. The mall has a very diverse range of shops—from a Christian bookstore to a specialty bakery ingredient store.

The Location:
It is very conveniently located near the MRT station.

The Verdict:
While the interior layout is a bit unique, the building itself is stunning. It is definitely worth a visit if you are passing by the area!

beautiful uniquely designed building. pretty for some, ugly for others, no middle path haha. the food are are good. agood mixture of fastfood cafe food, restaurent and street food like fun toast and Pontain Wanton noodles. Japan home and Red man has a big shop here.Good that there is a CS Fresh here too.

One Holland Village has quickly become one of my favourite lifestyle malls in Singapore. The entire place feels very open, modern, and lively, with a nice mix of indoor and outdoor spaces that fit the Holland Village atmosphere perfectly

What really stands out is how dog-friendly the mall is. You regularly see people walking their dogs around the open plaza areas, and the environment feels welcoming and relaxed compared to many traditional malls. There are often events, seasonal decorations, performances, and buskers in the central open space, which gives the whole area a vibrant community vibe, especially in the evenings

There are also plenty of food choices ranging from cafes and bakeries to Chinese cuisine and casual dining spots. The open seating areas make it easy to hang out with friends or simply relax and people-watch. I also really like the quieter rooftop-style area on the top floor, which feels surprisingly peaceful and provides a nice escape from the busier sections below

I've been to One Holland Village a few times now, and it's really growing on me. It’s quickly becoming one of my favorite spots to hang out, especially on weekends.

​The vibe here is fantastic. It has a nice open-air concept that feels very relaxed and spacious, unlike typical enclosed malls. It’s incredibly pet-friendly—I love seeing so many dog owners bringing their furry friends along to "shop" and walk around. It really adds a lively and community-focused atmosphere to the place.

​There are so many food options to choose from, ranging from casual bites to nice sit-down restaurants (like Gyukatsu Kyoto Katsugyu, Tsujiri, South Bank Fish & Chips etc.) Whether you want a full meal or just a dessert, you're covered.

​When I visited on a Sunday evening, there was "live" music playing, which set a really nice mood. It’s a great place to just chill, enjoy good food, and soak in the atmosphere. Highly recommend visiting!

My son would be absolutely thrilled if it were a giant fox. (Okay, the fox is AI-generated... the real installation is a balloon cat, which is actually quite cute and adds a lot of character to the area. But let’s be honest, a giant fox would be epic.)

Unique mall in Singapore, Spacious with Great architecture planning. Top Pet friendly mall in Sg. Lotsa options for food and good retail mix. Great ventilation. During my visit there are great busking performance.

One Holland Village, an outdoor concept lifestyle mall in an iconic district, is a pretty vibrant destination and chill hangout spot for both locals and visitors👍👏💪

Being walkable from Holland Village MRT Station on the Circle Line is a big plus! This open-air mall offers a good mix of dining, shopping, lifestyle options and services with community space that fosters connections and creativity; ideal for leisurely family day out and casual outing with friends. OHV boasts an array of eateries, from indie cafés to gourmet restaurants that diners and foodies can enjoy any time of the day👍👏💪

Recognized as one of the most pet-centric malls that feature pet-friendly F&B establishments, a dog park, and pet bakery, OHV makes a paw-tastic spot for both pet owners and their furkids👍👏💪

Sheng Siong Supermarket: A true local shopping experience.

This supermarket is tucked away and blends into the residential apartment building. As soon as I walked in, there was a strong smell of fresh meat, which is typical for a local market with a wet section.

The Layout & Selection:
The aisles are quite narrow, and the goods are packed very tightly throughout the shop. I was looking for Irvins Salted Egg Fish Skin, but they didn't have the snacks in stock—only the Irvins instant noodles.

The Service:
The staff was very helpful and friendly when I asked for assistance. Even though it was busy, they were kind.

The Verdict:
This is a standard neighborhood supermarket for locals rather than a luxury market for travelers. It’s a great place to see how Singaporeans do their daily shopping!
5 stars because it's 24/7, -1 star cuz Fairprice has better prices
Good place to pick up dry items but aviold the fresh food section . Very very  poor quality of fruits and vegetables .
Good and cheap goods priced for everyone. Great deals always.

One might wonder why there is no self payment kiosk here, but this is where this place stands out with the fast and helpful cashiers. Compassion, thoughtfulness and kindness is also what you can't get from self payment kiosks. Shop with a human touch here.

Visited Cold Storage at around 6:55 PM on 1 June 2026 and purchased a loaf of sourdough bread. As the store has an automatic bread-slicing machine, I politely asked a staff member named Jasmine  if she could help slice the bread.

Unfortunately, she responded with a blunt "No" in a rather rude manner. When questioned further she explained that she could not do it because the staff member responsible for the machine had already gone home. Given that the machine could have sliced the loaf in about a minute, the response felt unhelpful and dismissive.

Thankfully, the cashier Hwee Tin was very kind and went out of her way to assist us by slicing the bread herself. Her willingness to help turned an otherwise disappointing experience into a positive one.

I appreciate the cashier's excellent customer service, but I hope the store can ensure that staff like Jasmine are more courteous and willing to assist customers where possible.

This CS Fresh branch has ‘Craft Beer on Tap’ although Hoegaarden can hardly be considered as craft beer. Little Creatures was good though, at $7 for half pint. You need to pay at the normal queue before you can collect your beer. Would be great if the beers are served in glasses instead of plastic cups.

Medium-ish cold storage but the only supermarket near the Buona Vista MRT outside of Holland Village area. Has a small bread and cheese, roast meats and salads, the usual. Three checkout counters. Generally fine but not as extensive a selection as the larger ones.

Looks modern but there are a lot of cages with carton boxes stacked near the checkout making it look ugly. It is rather small and the range are limited: eg I can’t seem to find Ben And Jerry Vegan ice-cream here, can’t of out of touch for a top end supermarket. Lots of stuff out of stock or do not stock: yalkult grapes, Meiji chocolate milk, etc. But other than this, the ambience is great. The checkout lady can improve on her customer service skills too just like the other Cold Storages: position premium but do not bother to train their checkout girls.

Would like to confer my sincere thanks and appreciation to Mui Ling for helping me to recover my laptop. I had left my laptop in the store which is completely my fault. The laptop contains all the important data. Mui Ling kept it safe for two days and returned it to me as I had left it today. Had it not for her help, both the laptop and the data would have been lost, which would be a disaster. My thanks to Mui Ling and the Cold Storage team at Star Vista for your professionalism, integrity, honesty and top-notch customer service!

WOW! Viatman Minimart is absolutely INCREDIBLE! 😍 Every visit is a joy — the shop is spotless, perfectly organised, and the staff are unbelievably friendly and helpful. They make you feel like royalty the moment you walk in!

Their Shopee collection point is PURE PERFECTION — lightning-fast, smooth, and completely hassle-free. Honestly, I’ve never experienced such amazing service anywhere else. Every parcel pickup feels effortless, and every interaction leaves you smiling.

I cannot recommend this place enough — it’s hands down one of the BEST minimarts and collection points ever! If you haven’t been here yet, you’re seriously missing out. Absolute 5-star experience from start to finish — I’m obsessed!
So rare to be able to find a minimart that specializes in Vietnamese food in the west. Love it!
Many variety of seasonings, frozen food and all look fresh and new.
Pleasant shopping in this mini mart, the shop assistant was able to communicate on good English and explain to us the various sauces and products they sell. Highly recommended.
Great place for all your Vietnamese Grocery items!!!

Reviewing the prime for hall 2 as there is no landmark added there. A major upgrade from giant express. Way cleaner and sells more variety of items than giant. Mostly self check out now. And they also have ready made food which is very needed for students who are studying late and eat supper.

1 star for the kinda products you carry. This is the worst dumplings I’ve purchased ever. The skins just fell off the moment they touched boiling water. In fact they already looked bad before cooking, cracks all over with falling skins. Pls stop importing such low quality products. Given that already had bad experience purchasing supposedly ‘discounted’ price items with marked up retail price here.
Imported products with economical pricing! Hardly able to find in big supermarket chains.
Tis best place to get groceries in the campus... cheap n good variety!
Recently they renovated the shop and added more kinds of items, including some common vitamins and medicines. The supermarket is very helpful for ntu students and staffs

Male staff, wearing spectacles, has no customer service. Unfriendly, unapproachable. He made me feel as if i owe him a living - but I am just buying things there. I believe management needs to send them for customer service training.

chinese lady cashier of older age attended to me on 3/8/25 at 12.37pm. she was extremely rude and unprofessional, if i could give zero stars i would.

when asked to redeem a voucher for payment, she asked me if it was qr code or paywave. i answered that it was a barcode voucher to scan.

auntie pulled up the nets payment QR code and when showed her my voucher to redeem, she began grumbling and saying that she had asked me earlier if i wanted to use voucher (which i stated upfront). she grumbled multiple times that it would take very long to cancel the transaction.

in the first place, i have seen auntie around and know that she is a longtime worker in this store. i am sure she is aware of how vouchers are redeemed (can vouchers be redeemed via scanning the nets qr?).

if it was a honest mistake on her part, i do wish your staff can show more patience and a better attitude to their customers in future.

do better please 7 eleven. i can’t imagine a patient or relative visiting a loved one who is sick in NUH being treated as such.
Rude! Rude! Rude! The worst 7 Eleven I have ever visited. Indeed the older lady is Rude and so is the big guy in glasses. They should stay at home if they hate their work so much.

Chinese cashier lady of older age is extremely rude, choses customers to pick on, and friendly towards her favourite regular customers, this shows she has capability to be service-oriented but chooses not to. When about to show the staff pass; she scolded, 你要敢敢的给我看卡!! 不然我不会给你discount (YOU MUST BRAVELY SHOW ME PASS IF NOT I WONT GIVE YOU DISCOUNT). show or don’t show also she find problem with you. 7 eleven please retrain the staff that you hire because honestly the business will be negatively affected when service is poor.

The Cashier was so rude and unprofessional, big chinese guy with glasses. I bought something and I gave him a big note because obviously, I don't have any change? Then he proceeded to tsk at me at an annoying tone, my bad didn't know I was here to receive attitude. Anyway, then he proceeded to just leave the item when I needed a bag and was like "DONE ALREADY LA" in such a rude tone..
This place is quite convenient,24 7 open, the staff there are also nice and generous
Opens 24 hours love it and it's quite fully stocked when I went inside the store
Suggest to train staff in asking for yuu membership prior to payment
One of the few places to buy grocery stuff around. Very convenient.
Newly open.

Went to the outlet (Tagore) to place an order for CNY, to make sure that delivery would be in place for reunion dinner.

Food was delivered just before the scheduled delivery time, which was exactly what we needed. Quality was good and tasted fresh. The fishballs and beef balls were especially well received.

Will use them again next time we do a steamboat.

What a great wet market, combined with Sheng Siong, lots of independent small stores, food court. The prices are fantastic compare to NTUC Fair price at the wet market. There’s heap of hawker food too, especially busy were prata, chicken rice, Lor mee and bee hoon and curry chicken. Nice atmosphere in the weekend and old nostalgia where your ah-Ma would love to shop here. Just wished it had more meat and vegetable stores in the wet market section. Good place to show the kids and spend the morning in the weekend.

I highly recommend this butcher!  Prime Pork ribs are very high quality, smooth, tender and fresh.  Their minced pork is the perfect meat/ fat ratio, very fresh.  No rancid smell at all.  I’ve also purchased their deboned chicken (quarter) 500gm pack many times- used it for grilling, steaming, braising.  Also recommend their pork meat balls- very tasty, has cuttlefish in it.  They deliver the items in an insulated box, full of ice packs to keep the meat cold and safe.  Everything is vacuum packed.  I’m so pleased with their service.  Their pricing is also competitive.  The thing with going to the wet market physically, you still need to repack into your desired portions upon coming home, so ordering from quan shui really saves me a lot of time and energy.

I always love going to the wet markets but cannot afford the time to wake up super early to get the freshest produce! Love that they deliver to your doorstep and even give the option to recycle the ice packs/boxes. The selection of produce is great, quality is really good and worth every bit of the slight premium!! Fruits & meat are super fresh! Ready to eat section is great for quick meals too! 100% will recommend!

We cooked with the wild caught balai threadfin for porridge, Silverfern short rib shabu shabu for Korean beef Bulgogi, silverfern ribeye steak for Beef bowl. It was amazing! 👌

We’ve been buying from Quan Shui Wet Market for a few times, and each time a satisfying one. Their items are fresh and clean, reached us in good condition and packaging. Complimentary delivery is reliable and good! Joyce from customer service is friendly and helpful. So far we’ve purchased almost all types of what they are selling and we’ll still purchase from them for long. Thank you Quan Shui Market!

Classic neighbourhood market with a strong heartland vibe.

It’s a sheltered market street with many stalls selling fresh produce, fruits, household items & small snacks. Very lively in the mornings, with residents doing their daily shopping & grabbing quick bites.

Convenient, down-to-earth & full of character
Love the hustle and bustle of the place. The energy and vibe is alluring. But it's hot during the afternoon
A very good market place, selling almost all your needs, fruits, snacks, nick-nacks, prices are very competitive. A good place to shop.

This is my favourite market in Singapore! My mother loves the fresh mee (egg noodles) and tofu sold in one of the market stalls. There are two food courts side-by-side so you will be spoilt for choice! There are POSB ATMs and a POSB branch there. There are also OCBC ATMs and a OCBC branch near Giant. Giant is perfect for those who need quick groceries at a low price. Your everyday needs are stocked there! There are a wide range of products for young and old sold at affordable prices in the market. Everyone can have fun! If you want your food fresh come at around 6 in the morning! Vegetables and meat would have just come in, and food would start being prepared. If you want a new and fun experience, try Bangkit market! It isn’t that crowded so if you practise social distancing, you’ll be safe!
There is a grocery shopping street which you will be able to get some good bargain.

Large outlet a 10 min walk from Buona MRT. For a saturday afternoon at an office area, there’s still quite some people but not too packed and I could get the benches and machines I needed.

Has all the machines you need, racks, cable jungle, cardio etc. Funnily enough you’ll find another standalone cable station, smith and half-rack at the functional area. I appreciate how they have both leg press and hack squat machines when many AF only have the leg press.

Most equipment are well maintained and smooth which is important.

Will definitely come back again if i’m around the area and it’s off-peak

The location is not that accessible but space is rather adequate. Almost all the machines you need are here, but unfortunately the machines are too close together. The functional area is very spacious though and I thought space here could be better utilised. Some of the weights are rather old and mixed brands. The doorway to the toilet is a hazard right next to a rack with uneven steps but I guess regulars will probably not have an issue with this.

This gym is well equipped for a 24hr gym, with lifting platforms and racks, big range of machines, cardio equipment, free weights, calisthenics and more. Equipment is in good shape and there are even Eleiko bars.

It has showers and lockers. 2 minute sheltered walk from BV MRT. Good value for money in Singapore and can access other AF gyms.

But what puts it above other gyms is customer service. My lifting belt was lost in the gym and the team got it back to me within 5 working days after going through CCTV even during their busy end of month reconciliation.

I have always had positive interactions with the gym staff and management at this AF branch. Keep it up!

Dear owner,
Last night at around 10pm plus I was at the gym and it was shocking to see that there were DOGS in the gym!!! It was a massive dog and a s/m size one. The owners of the dogs were not working out but walking around with the dogs and even sat down near the dumbbell area. I am not sure if they were members or friends of members or staff or family / friend to the owners of AFBV because they acted like it’s a very normal thing to do. There were there for a long period going in and out of the gym with the dogs. This Is absolutely unacceptable as it’s;
1. Unhygienic.
2. Some ppl have allergies to furry pets.
3. Totally disrespectful to certain religious members that cannot come into contact with dogs.
4. Very disruptive for people going there to gym.

The dogs last night was definitely not service dogs. I suggest you review the CCTV camera and take immediate action. The whole gym should be sanitised! Photos shown below are not the only area the dogs sat, they were inside the main gym area with owners sitting down - you can review the CCTV to get a better idea.

Usually very crowded and rather cramped. Not the best experience especially with the water dispenser remain not fixed for months.

Other than that most machines are present and in good order.

4pm 16 November Sunday, went in to community club to ask to print a medical document which was 3 pieces.

Went to the help desk to speak to a male staff(middle age). Made sure that I was standing in front of him pointing to printer, asking him for help as I need it.

Rejected me 3 times. Nowhere around entire premises/neighbourhood could print. I DIDNT REALISE COMMUNITY CLUB is not for community simple help. Could have easily print but chose not to. He didn’t even thought about it and just reject. The feeling was horrible. Joke on how PA has such compassion when compassion is part of your motto.

If SG Community Clubs are now trained to be like this, please close down and save our tax money. Useless representative for someone working FOR the community.

Please check your CCTV. Rough time given to you. Email me if required, I can point out the person.

Great condition, fkoor not slippery and good ventilation yet not windy. So much better compared to Frontier CC. Water cooler is nearby and cold too!

Minus 1 star because ceiling too low haha

Small cosy community center serving the residents nearby. Center even has its own Mac! Courses offered by center are wide ranging and inexpensive. There are festive programs offered time to time that attracts lots of participation.
A community public place for all. You have event hall and can convert to basketball court. Facilities like badminton games can be play here as well.

The area is great for doing exercises..I usually do my morning walk along Jurong West st42 canal facing PIE, and then some exercises that are being put up by the relevant authorities.. Lately I found some dead branches that have fallen from the giant banana trees and coconut trees, and for more than a week, they are not been removed.. I hope the relevant authorities will be looking at these both unsafe and poor environment cleanliness...and quickly remove them away.

Name is a little misleading as nothing here is about firefly unless the graffiti. Many kids playing basketball. Minus 2 stars due to inaccessible designs. Many knee level walls built in the estate to prevent global access to the park. Only through a small walk way as entrance. As such, I have to jump down the short wall which may break my knees. Imagine elderly. Built in a new estate.

Love this park, and the pleasant and relaxing atmosphere surrounded by beautiful greenery. Some common floral and fauna spotted.
Different floral at different times of the year.
Good place for hanging out. Air is really fresh, it has a basketball court and playground. We had a class party at Firefly park. Kids friendly. Really good place.
Pleasant park with 2 playgrounds, trampolines, a basketball court, 2 fitness corners, small running track and an amphitheater!

There is a large playground in this park, with an elderly exercise area and a basketball court. This is a good place for the residents to exercise and have fun and that this park has facilities for all age group to enjoy. However, there is no toilet in the park so you need to go to the nearby food court for toilet. Overall a good park and I have fun here enjoying the scenery and playing at the basketball court with my friends.
A peaceful and beautiful spot to unwind. The calm waters and greenery make it perfect for a relaxing walk or quiet moment away from the city. Definitely a great place to recharge.

We came here for a family photoshoot because this couple had taken their photos under the Love Tree when they were newly married. Now, with their daughter, we returned to the same spot. It was a cool afternoon with fresh air, a light drizzle, and a beautiful sunset afterwards. Everything felt calm and serene—and the results were simply amazing!

Good place to stroll and have a picnic. Sunset view is beautiful as well.
Clean toilets, shelter, parking, and benches are available.
A great place to have a stroll and for fishing. Nice view of greenery and trees of the nearby golf course.

A beautiful park for your morning and evening walk. Facing the nice reservoir view, rain trees keeping the place cool and sheltered.  Nice cool breeze and sunset view. Some people doing their fishing here. Unblock view of the scenery.  A great place to chill out.

Lower Seletar Reservoir Park is such a peaceful and scenic spot in Singapore! 🌅 The waterfront views are beautiful, especially during sunrise and sunset, and the park is very well maintained. It’s perfect for evening strolls, jogging, cycling, or just relaxing by the water. The jetty and heritage boardwalk add a special charm, and the cool breeze from the reservoir makes it even more refreshing. A great place to unwind and enjoy nature — highly recommended! 🌿✨

The park is beautifully maintained, with a delightful variety of flowers that add color and charm to every corner. Each pathway feels inviting, filled with vibrant blooms and natural scents that make the walk feel almost therapeutic.

The gardens are especially peaceful—perfect for an evening stroll after a long day. Whether you want to unwind, enjoy nature, or simply spend some quiet time alone, this park offers the ideal atmosphere. It’s a serene escape from the noise of the city, and I’d highly recommend it to anyone looking for a calm and scenic place to relax.

10/10, would absolutely paddle back for this again. 🌅🚣‍♂️

The sunrise kayak + breakfast tour was such a peaceful, feel-good start to the day. Ali and Shiva were fantastic, friendly, funny, and genuinely informative. They made everything feel easy and welcoming, and were always ready to help while keeping the whole experience light and enjoyable.

It’s one of those rare activities that’s genuinely for everyone. Easy to pick up, surprisingly calming, and somehow makes you feel like you’ve got your life together before 9am. Paddle a little, enjoyed a lot, then reward yourself with breakfast by the water… what’s not to love?

Seas the day, eat your way through it, repeat. Try it!

A Serene Escape, Even in a Light Drizzle

The Equatorial showers in this heartland park is a gem, especially in light rain. I recently went for a walk as a gentle shower began, and it was a surprisingly perfect experience. The rain kept the crowds away, and the sound of drops on the canopy and the reservoir's surface was incredibly peaceful.

The best part was watching the water itself. Each raindrop created a cascade of tiny, expanding ripples across the entire reservoir. It was a mesmerizing and deeply calming effect, turning the whole scene into a living, moving work of art.

It’s a wonderful reminder that you don't need to travel far for a moment of tranquility. A short, refreshing walk here, even under a light rain, is a perfect way to reset.

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
Lower Seletar Reservoir Park is such a peaceful and scenic spot in Singapore! 🌅 The waterfront views are beautiful, especially during sunrise and sunset, and the park is very well maintained. It’s perfect for evening strolls, jogging, cycling, or just relaxing by the water. The jetty and heritage boardwalk add a special charm, and the cool breeze from the reservoir makes it even more refreshing. A great place to unwind and enjoy nature — highly recommended! 🌿✨
Many birds and nice places to explore  nature. About 2km+ with proper walking path. Must bring insects repellent, many many mosquitos
Kranji gate is better reached by cycling or driving as roads do not have sidewalks

Super quaint and ulu place where most of the visitors are bird watchers (run into a few during the visit). Quite hard to reach because closest bus stops (let alone train stations) are kilometers away. Walking to the location is also hard since the road has no pavement. The upside is that it's free of charge and the observation tower at the end of the trail gives a nice vantage point over the bird reserve and Kranji Reservoir nearby. Maintenance works could've been better since some of the sheds seem to be quite run down.
Very quiet place with few visitors. Smell of animal poo, probably some animal farm nearby.

Kranji Marshes offers a unique escape into nature, but it's a mixed bag in terms of maintenance and experience. Located in Singapore, the marshes are a good option for those looking to immerse themselves in a quieter, more natural environment away from the hustle and bustle of the city.

The walking trail is decent, making for a pleasant trek, and I found that a 30-minute walk provided an opportunity to appreciate the surrounding flora and fauna. However, the lack of upkeep was evident. Some paths were overgrown, and the area felt somewhat neglected, which detracted from the overall experience.

One significant downside was the mosquito situation. I ended up with 18 bites during my visit, so I recommend bringing insect repellent or wear long sleeves and pants if you decide to go. The marshes can feel quite deserted, which might appeal to some seeking solitude, but it could also be a bit unsettling for others.

Overall, Kranji Marshes has potential for a refreshing nature walk, but be prepared for a few inconveniences along the way. If you are willing to overlook the maintenance issues and the pesky mosquitoes, it can be a worthwhile retreat into Singapore's natural landscape.
Lovely, well grown water plants and very green foliage . clean water and a lot tortles and small fishes .
Nice and peaceful
Turtles!
Beautiful, clean, and well-maintained park. Highly recommended.
The pond with all the turtles on the other side of the building is really nice.

Silver Garden at HortPark is one of the best highlights of this peaceful green space in Alexandra. Known for its striking silver, grey, and pale-green foliage, it offers a visually refreshing experience. A notable feature is the polished metal artwork displayed among the greenery, adding a modern, artistic contrast to the garden’s natural surroundings. A great spot for a leisurely stroll or photography—definitely worth visiting when exploring HortPark.
From far away, really looks like frosted silvery leaves with whitish hues which was a delightful surprise since we're in tropical climate
Nice garden inside Hort Park. Interesting plants and flowers to see. Good to see the greenery in the middle of huge buildings
Amazing walk in the beautiful place
A much cleaner park than I remembered it was 20 years back. It’s really windy till I developed a running nose in the mid afternoon. Great place to chill as not so crowded like east coast park.
Quite nice place. Just the beach needs a bit of cleaning. Saw people fishing and wind surfing with sails. And even a bird catch fish. Not to mention airplanes taking off from Changi airport.

A nice place to be quiet, reflect, and be with loved ones, bustling with children and people cycling. There are also people fishing, selling things, everything is orderly and nice, not far from Changi Village when I was here, ill come back here in the future.

A 7am visit to Changi Beach Park offers a perfectly serene and quiet stroll, feeling like a well-kept secret from the bustling city. As the sun begins to rise, casting a soft, golden glow over the sand and sea, the park is enveloped in a peaceful silence. The only early birds are the dedicated park cleaners, whose quiet work in the morning calm adds to the tranquil atmosphere rather than disturbs it.

The true magic, however, is the unique soundscape. The tranquility is gently broken every few minutes by the deep, distant rumble of aircraft—a powerful yet strangely soothing reminder of the world in motion as planes make their final descent into the nearby Changi International Airport. It’s a captivating contrast that makes this simple morning walk feel both secluded and connected to something larger.

My family's favourite beach. Lovely sun, sand, sea and sometimes jellyfish. Lol..
Nice long stretch of beach to the roundabout point for that Instagramable picture with the finger.

Toilet amenities are OK. If you're hungry for bites, changi village hawker centre is only 10minutes walk away. Parking on weekends can be very limited due to the trending wingfoiling sport.

And oh.. not supposed to tie anything to trees now. No hammocks unless self standing.

I have been wanting to come here for a while. There is no convininent public transport, so one have to calculate the time and effort they need to reach here by foot, cycling, or being dropped by right at the entrance. The area itself is steep, with many ups and downs, make it quite challenging for biking and walking for those who are not that fit. There are signs along the way that prohibit anyone from taking pictures, so I think you have to be careful with that.

There are two sunrise spots on the map, although I think it can be lumped into one, because I only see one significant area, the rest are like walking inside the light wood on a concrete path.

The sign also says there shouldn’t be any motorized vehicle, but there were one or two electric bikes whose owners didn’t bother to slow down at all. This could be a good short cut to connect to the Mandai Road if you are fit enough to walk/cycle.

There is no seat along the way, not one, nothing. If you need a rest, the best bet is to sit on a big rock that is far and between or just sit on the concrete.

Nice and peaceful place to enjoy nature and exercise. Do take note that pass the Mandai depot area, there will be no lighting, so plan your activities before the sunset. Good place to cycle also tho, certain part maybe difficult due to the rising slope.
Loved coming here at 6.30am!! You can see mist just before/when he sun comes up 😍 Wear proper shoes for better grip and proper clothes to protect yourself from the tall grass and buggsss 🐜🐛🐞

I try to go here on 6pm evening，lol. A bit difficult to find the place. The road was no one it make me creepy and scary when walk alone on around evening 6pm. Maybe next time I woke up in early morning to see the sunrise.

The actual connector is actually quite a short distance but it takes you to different parks which is awesome! The entrance is near the SLE flyover. Nice greenery, would come here again during sun rise cos I believe it would be beautiful. For walking, it’s pretty easy as its pavement all the way.  I walked through the connector and ended up at Mandai and it was only a short few minutes walk to Bird Paradise. Will definitely walk this path again to go to the Mandai park connector and Lower Seletar Reservoir.
